      // pick up whatever stale value was in `stats` at openFullPage time.
      // Two-pass _unlockedCount for palette threshold achievements.
      try {
        fresh._unlockedCount = computeUnlockedIds(fresh).length;
      } catch { fresh._unlockedCount = stats._unlockedCount || 0; }
      return fresh;
    }

    /**
     * Sync variant of buildFreshStats for use in sync handlers where
     * `await` would be disruptive (e.g. accent repaint on settings-
     * change, which fires many times per session). Reads the cheap
     * localStorage-backed stats synchronously and carries forward the
     * LAST-KNOWN value of any async-derived field (currently
     * eventsResponded) from `stats`. Call sites that genuinely need
     * a just-fetched eventsResponded should use the async form.
     *
     * @returns {object}
     */
    function buildFreshStatsSync() {
      const fresh = { ...stats };
      try { fresh.counters = getCounters(); } catch { fresh.counters = stats.counters || {}; }
      try { fresh.streaks  = getStreaks();  } catch { fresh.streaks  = stats.streaks  || { current: 0, longest: 0 }; }
      // eventsResponded is async (dynamic-imported participation.js) so
      // we carry the init-time value; callers that change event state
      // (completing a prompt in the profile) should use buildFreshStats()
      // instead. The TYPICAL sync-path caller (accent / title repaint
      // on a flair change) doesn't mutate events, so stale is safe.
      fresh.eventsResponded = stats.eventsResponded || 0;
      // Carry forward _unlockedCount for palette threshold criteria.
      fresh._unlockedCount = stats._unlockedCount || 0;
      return fresh;
    }

    try {
      unlockedIds = computeUnlockedIds(stats);
    } catch {
      unlockedIds = [];
    }
    // Two-pass unlock: palette_vellum / palette_silver / palette_deep
    // gate on _unlockedCount (total achievements earned). The first
    // pass above didn't have _unlockedCount, so inject it and re-run
    // to pick up any palette thresholds that just crossed.
    stats._unlockedCount = unlockedIds.length;
    try {
      unlockedIds = computeUnlockedIds(stats);
    } catch { /* keep first-pass result */ }

    // Opening the profile counts as acknowledgment of any pending notifications.
    // This fires the settings-changed event, which causes the mini-card to
    // re-render without its pulse state. We don't require the user to scroll
    // to the relevant section — opening is the acknowledgment.
    try { markAchievementsSeen(unlockedIds); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
    // Record the first-detected unlock date for any new achievement. This
    // populates notifications.unlockDates so the achievements grid can
    // show "Unlocked: 3d ago" on hover.
    try { recordUnlockDates(unlockedIds); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
    // Acknowledge BOTH week and day — the user is seeing the current state,
    // and if they toggle cadence later we don't want stale pulse-pending on
    // the mode they weren't using.
    try { markWeekSeen(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
    try { markDaySeen(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Personal-best detection. Compares current stats against stored
    // peaks, updates peaks if any improved. The IMPROVEMENTS themselves
    // are no longer surfaced as pop-up celebration toasts — the mini-card
    // pulse (wider when pendingCount increases) now handles "hey come
    // look" and the personal-bests chips inside the splash reveal what
    // changed once the profile is open. Keeps the "friendly neighbor
    // waving you over" energy the user asked for, rather than bolting on
    // a distracting toast stack over their chat.
    //
    // We still call checkAndUpdateBests for its side effect — persisting
    // new peak values. That state feeds the chips inside the profile,
    // which is where the reveal now lives.
    try { checkAndUpdateBests(stats); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Weekly activity summary. We still call checkSummary for its side
    // effect (resetting the weekly-snapshot window + updating the
    // activity-summary section data), but no longer surface it as a
    // toast. The user will see the summary in the activity section
    // when they open the profile. Same rationale as personal bests:
    // pulse to invite, splash to reveal.
    try {
      const counters = (() => { try { return getCounters(); } catch { return {}; } })();
      checkSummary(counters);
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    const activeEvents = getActiveEvents();
    try { markEventsSeen(activeEvents.map(ev => ev.id)); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Current prompts + completion state. Cadence determines how many
    // prompts show and what key drives selection. Either way, completions
    // bucket into the containing week (stored under completedByWeek) so
    // cadence switching leaves history intact.
    const weekKey = getCurrentWeekKey();
    const cadence = (settings && settings.prompts && settings.prompts.cadence) || 'weekly';
    let livePrompts;
    let dayKey = null;
    if (cadence === 'daily') {
      dayKey = getCurrentDayKey();
      const dayPrompt = getDayPrompt(dayKey);
      livePrompts = dayPrompt ? [dayPrompt] : [];
    } else {
      livePrompts = getWeekPrompts(weekKey);
    }
    const completedIds = getCompletedIds(weekKey);

    const profile = (settings && settings.profile) || {};
    const displayState = (settings && settings.display && settings.display.sections) || {};

    // ---- splash ----
    const xp = xpFromStats(stats);
    const lvl = levelFromXP(xp);

    const splash = createSplash({
      onCardClick: async () => {
        // One-click share: build link → copy to clipboard → toast.
        let freshSettings = settings;
        try { freshSettings = loadSettings(); } catch { /* keep stale */ }
        let freshUnlocked = unlockedIds;
        let freshArchetype = null;
        try {
          const freshStats = await buildFreshStats();
          freshUnlocked = computeUnlockedIds(freshStats);
          try { freshArchetype = getPrimaryArchetype(freshStats); } catch { /* null */ }
        } catch { /* fall back */ }
        const accent = resolveActiveAccent(freshSettings, stats, freshUnlocked);
        const p = (freshSettings && freshSettings.profile) || {};
        const tc = (p && p.themeColors) || {};
        try {
          const shareVm = toShareViewModel({
            displayName: p.displayName || p.username || 'Chronicler',
            title: deriveTitle(freshUnlocked, freshSettings),
            archetype: freshArchetype,
            level: lvl.level,
            accent: accent.color,
            vellum: tc.vellum || '#e8dcc4',
            silver: tc.silver || '#8b95a3',
            secondary: tc.secondary || '#142847',
            primary: tc.primary || '#0d1b2e',
            pinnedBadges: pickPinnedBadges(freshUnlocked, 5),
            xpIntoLevel: lvl.xpIntoLevel,
            xpForNextLevel: lvl.xpForNextLevel,
            progress01: lvl.progress01,
            wordsWritten: stats.wordsWritten || 0,
            threadCount: stats.threadCount || 0,
            daysActive: stats.daysActive || 0,
          });
          const code = encodeShareCode(shareVm);
          const shareUrl = buildShareUrl(code);
          if (navigator && navigator.clipboard && navigator.clipboard.writeText) {
            await navigator.clipboard.writeText(shareUrl);
          } else {
            const tmp = document.createElement('textarea');
            tmp.value = shareUrl;
            tmp.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;opacity:0;';
            document.body.appendChild(tmp);
            tmp.select();
            document.execCommand('copy');
            tmp.remove();
          }
          showToast('Copied to clipboard!', { ms: 2000, kind: 'info' });
          bumpCounter('shareCardOpens');
        } catch (e) {
          console.warn('[pf] share copy failed:', e && e.message);
          showToast('Copy failed — try again', { ms: 2000, kind: 'info' });
        }
      },
    });

    function refreshSplashFromSettings() {
      let freshSettings = settings;
      try { freshSettings = loadSettings(); } catch { /* keep stale */ }
      const p = (freshSettings && freshSettings.profile) || {};

      // Re-compute unlocks with current prompt stats — completing a prompt
      // mid-overlay-session should update pinned badges and title if it
      // crossed an achievement threshold.
      let freshUnlocked = unlockedIds;
      try {
        // Prompt-stats change mid-session when the user checks a prompt
        // inside the profile, so fold them in from freshSettings. The
        // rest of the bundle (counters/streaks/eventsResponded) comes
        // from the helper. Sync form is fine here — this runs on every
        // settings-changed event (many times/session) and eventsResponded
        // doesn't shift without an explicit event-prompt completion,
        // which the splash-refresh path doesn't perform.
        const freshStats = { ...buildFreshStatsSync(), ...computePromptStats(freshSettings) };
        freshUnlocked = computeUnlockedIds(freshStats);
      } catch { /* fall back to initial unlock list */ }

      // Re-derive archetype on every refresh so a threshold crossing
      // (e.g., just completed the streak that pushes them into Daily)
      // reflects live in the splash.
      let freshArchetype = null;
      try {
        freshArchetype = getPrimaryArchetype(buildFreshStatsSync());
      } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

      splash.update({
        displayName: p.displayName || p.username || 'Chronicler',
        avatarUrl: p.avatarUrl || null,
        title: deriveTitle(freshUnlocked, freshSettings),
        archetype: freshArchetype,
        level: lvl.level,
        xpIntoLevel: lvl.xpIntoLevel,
        xpForNextLevel: lvl.xpForNextLevel,
        progress01: lvl.progress01,
        pinnedBadges: pickPinnedBadges(freshUnlocked, 6),
      });

      // Re-apply flair colors — the user may have just picked a new
      // accent, text color, or meta color in the Details form. overlay
      // is declared up-front as `let overlay = null`, so the truthiness
      // check is safe on the first synchronous call (before createOverlay
      // returns) — we just skip. applyAllFlair() below handles initial paint.
      if (overlay && overlay.style) {
        // Re-paint all flair channels (accent, vellum, silver) on the
        // overlay + :root. paintAllChannels handles the upstream cascade
        // for accent (notification banner, links, thread highlight).
        try {
          paintAllChannels(freshSettings, stats, freshUnlocked, overlay);
        } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
      }

      // Rebuild the Prompts section body when the cadence setting changes.
      // Previously we closed the overlay to force a reload; that slammed
      // shut on the user as they were toggling Daily/Weekly. Now we just
      // re-render the body in place — no overlay flicker, no context loss.
      const freshCadence = (freshSettings && freshSettings.prompts && freshSettings.prompts.cadence) || 'weekly';
      if (freshCadence !== currentCadence) {
        currentCadence = freshCadence;
        let newPrompts;
        let newDayKey = null;
        if (freshCadence === 'daily') {
          newDayKey = getCurrentDayKey();
          const dayPrompt = getDayPrompt(newDayKey);
          newPrompts = dayPrompt ? [dayPrompt] : [];
        } else {
          newPrompts = getWeekPrompts(weekKey);
        }
        const newBody = createPromptsBody({
          weekKey,
          prompts: newPrompts,
          completedIds: getCompletedIds(weekKey),
          activeEvents,
          cadence: freshCadence,
        });
        const bodyWrap = promptsSection.querySelector('.pf-section-body');
        if (bodyWrap) {
          bodyWrap.replaceChildren(newBody);
          // Re-mount daily quests into the freshly created prompts list.
          try {
            const pl = newBody.querySelector('.pf-prompts-list');
            if (pl) initDailyQuest(pl);
          } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
        }
      }
    }
    // Track the mounted cadence so the listener can detect changes.
    let currentCadence = cadence;
    refreshSplashFromSettings();

    // Live-refresh the splash when the user changes avatar / title / display
    // name inside the Details form. Cleaned up when the overlay closes.
    const unsubscribe = onSettingsChange(refreshSplashFromSettings);

    // Focus mode extras — rendered in the overlay flow but hidden by CSS
    // until focus mode is entered. Splash on its own is generic; this card
    // carries the user's "writing fingerprint" (radar) plus four corner
    // stat chips so the screenshot has texture to read.
    const shareChips = createShareChips({
      level: lvl.level,
      unlockedIds,
      achievements: ACHIEVEMENTS,
      promptsCompleted: stats.promptsCompletedTotal || 0,
    });
    const focusExtras = h('div', { class: 'pf-focus-extras' }, [
      shareChips.topRow,
      createWritingRadar({ stats }),
      shareChips.bottomRow,
      createActivitySparkline({
        currentWeekCompletedCount: completedIds.size,
      }),
    ]);

    // ---- sections ----
    const aboutSection = createSection({
      id: 'about',
      title: 'About',
      children: createAboutBody({ initialValue: profile.bio }),
      initialState: displayState.about,
    });

    const detailsSection = createSection({
      id: 'details',
      title: 'Details',
      children: createDetailsBody({ profile, unlockedIds, stats }),
      initialState: displayState.details,
    });

    // "Edit Prompts" button — opens the quest theme editor modal.
    // openEditPrompts is in the same IIFE scope (daily_quest.js).
    const editPromptsBtn = h('button', {
      type: 'button',
      class: 'pf-section-action',
      onClick: () => {
        try { openEditPrompts(); } catch { /* non-fatal if daily_quest not loaded */ }
      },
    }, ['Edit Prompts']);

    const promptsSection = createSection({
      id: 'prompts',
      title: 'Prompts',
      children: createPromptsBody({
        weekKey,
        prompts: livePrompts,
        completedIds,
        activeEvents,
        cadence,
      }),
      initialState: displayState.prompts,
      headerActions: [editPromptsBtn],
    });

    const archiveSection = createSection({
      id: 'archive',
      title: 'Prompt Archive',
      children: createPromptArchive(),
      initialState: displayState.archive,
      onToggled: ({ collapsed }) => {
        // Bump only on EXPAND (user is opening the archive), not on
        // collapse. Counter reflects "views" of the archive.
        if (!collapsed) bumpCounter('promptArchiveOpens');
      },
    });

    const chronicleSection = createSection({
      id: 'chronicle',
      title: 'Chronicle',
      children: createChronicleGrid({ stats }),
      initialState: displayState.chronicle,
    });

    const styleSection = createSection({
      id: 'style',
      title: 'Writing Style',
      children: createWritingRadar({ stats }),
      initialState: displayState.style,
    });

    const achievementsSection = createSection({
      id: 'achievements',
      title: 'Achievements',
      children: createAchievementsGrid({ unlockedIds, unlockDates: getUnlockDates() }),
      initialState: displayState.achievements,
    });

    // ---- activity counters ----
    // Shows per-feature usage counts tracked by stats/counters.js.
    // Surfaces data that users have been accumulating silently; also
    // establishes visibility for the future tiered-achievement system
    // that will unlock based on these same counters.
    const counters = getCounters();

    // Per-thread breakdown (#3) needs human-readable thread names. We
    // resolve them here BEFORE handing off to createActivityBody so the
    // builder stays pure-sync. Two layers of best-effort:
    //   - if window.db / threads table missing → empty map (builder
    //     falls back to 'Thread #<id>' for every entry)
    //   - if a specific thread was deleted upstream since the counter
    //     was tagged → that id stays unresolved (same fallback)
    // We resolve the union of ids across ALL per-thread counters so
    // every breakdown row in the strip can render names without
    // additional fetches.
    const threadNamesById = {};
    try {
      const byThread = getCountersByThread();
      const ids = Object.keys(byThread)
        .map(s => Number(s))
        .filter(n => Number.isFinite(n));
      if (ids.length > 0 && typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.db && window.db.threads) {
        // Use bulkGet when available (Dexie supports it), fall back to
        // serial gets if not. Either way we tolerate per-id failures.
        let rows = [];
        if (typeof window.db.threads.bulkGet === 'function') {
          rows = await window.db.threads.bulkGet(ids).catch(() => []);
        } else {
          rows = await Promise.all(
            ids.map(id => window.db.threads.get(id).catch(() => null))
          );
        }
        for (const row of rows || []) {
          if (row && row.id != null && typeof row.name === 'string' && row.name) {
            threadNamesById[String(row.id)] = row.name;
          }
        }
        // Opportunistic prune of stale per-thread counters (#3 follow-up).
        // We just enumerated live threads anyway — pass the live set so
        // pruneCountersByThread can drop entries for threads the user
        // deleted upstream. Bounded growth without a separate scan.
        // Build the live set from rows that came back non-null, regardless
        // of whether they had a name; non-null === thread still exists.
        const liveSet = new Set(
          (rows || [])
            .filter(r => r && r.id != null)
            .map(r => String(r.id))
        );
        try { pruneCountersByThread(liveSet); } catch { /* best-effort */ }
      }
    } catch { /* best-effort; empty map → fallback labels in builder */ }

    const activitySection = createSection({
      id: 'activity',
      title: 'Activity',
      children: createActivityBody({
        counters,
        streaks: (() => { try { return getStreaks(); } catch { return null; } })(),
        streakStatus: (() => { try { return streakStatus(); } catch { return 'broken'; } })(),
        threadNamesById,
      }),
      initialState: displayState.activity || { collapsed: false, blurred: false },
    });

    const backupSection = createSection({
      id: 'backup',
      title: 'Backup',
      children: createBackupBody(),
      initialState: displayState.backup,
    });

    // ---- Copyright footer ----
    const footer = document.createElement('div');
    footer.className = 'pf-footer';

    const copyright = document.createElement('span');
    copyright.textContent = '© 2026 therealwestninja.';

    const deviantLink = document.createElement('a');
    deviantLink.href = 'https://www.deviantart.com/west-ninja';
    deviantLink.target = '_blank';
    deviantLink.rel = 'noopener';
    deviantLink.textContent = 'DeviantArt';
    deviantLink.className = 'pf-footer-link';

    const githubLink = document.createElement('a');
    githubLink.href = 'https://github.com/therealwestninja';
    githubLink.target = '_blank';
    githubLink.rel = 'noopener';
    githubLink.textContent = 'GitHub';
    githubLink.className = 'pf-footer-link';

    const sep1 = document.createTextNode(' · ');
    const sep2 = document.createTextNode(' · ');

    footer.appendChild(copyright);
    footer.appendChild(sep1);
    footer.appendChild(deviantLink);
    footer.appendChild(sep2);
    footer.appendChild(githubLink);

    // ---- overlay ----
    //
    // `overlay` was declared `let overlay = null` at the top of
    // openFullPage() so refreshSplashFromSettings(), which fires
    // synchronously during init, can safely test `if (overlay)`
    // without a TDZ violation. Assign exactly once, here.
    overlay = createOverlay({
      ariaLabel: 'Your profile',
      onClose: () => { try { unsubscribe(); } catch {} },
      children: [
        splash,
        focusExtras,
        aboutSection,
        detailsSection,
        promptsSection,
        archiveSection,
        chronicleSection,
        styleSection,
        achievementsSection,
        activitySection,
        backupSection,
        footer,
      ],
    });

    // Apply the user's chosen flair colors (accent, text, meta) as CSS
    // custom properties on both the overlay and :root. If the user
    // changes any color mid-session, refreshSplashFromSettings (wired
    // to onSettingsChange) re-calls this.
    function applyAllFlair() {
      let freshSettings = settings;
      try { freshSettings = loadSettings(); } catch { /* keep stale */ }
      let freshUnlocked = unlockedIds;
      try {
        freshUnlocked = computeUnlockedIds(buildFreshStatsSync());
      } catch { /* fall back */ }
      paintAllChannels(freshSettings, stats, freshUnlocked, overlay);
    }
    applyAllFlair();

    overlay.show();

    // Daily quests mount above .pf-prompts-list. Pass the element
    // directly from the captured promptsSection rather than searching
    // the whole document — avoids timing issues with DOM mounting.
    try {
      const pl = promptsSection.querySelector('.pf-prompts-list');
      if (pl) initDailyQuest(pl);
    } catch (e) {
      console.warn('[pf] initDailyQuest:', e && e.message);
    }
  }

  /* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     module: profile/index.js
     ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  // profile/index.js
  //
  // Top-level wiring. Brings together:
  //   - stats/db.js    — read upstream IndexedDB
  //   - stats/queries.js — pure stat computation
  //   - achievements/tiers.js — XP/level math
  //   - render/mini_card.js — the DOM element
  //   - profile/mount.js — sidebar injection
  //
  // This module is the one "main" for the project — everything else is a pure
  // helper. Keep it small: if logic lives here and it grows past ~100 lines,
  // extract it.




















  const REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS = 30_000;

  /**
   * Build a mini-card view model from current stats + profile settings.
   *
   * `isFreshlyIncreased` is true when pendingCount just bumped up from the
   * previous refresh's value — the mini-card uses that signal to throw a
   * brief, more attention-getting "hey look over here" pulse on top of its
   * ambient pending pulse. See mini_card.js for the render-time wiring.
   */
  function buildViewModel(stats, profile, pendingCount = 0, isFreshlyIncreased = false) {
    const xp = xpFromStats(stats);
    const lvl = levelFromXP(xp);
    return {
      displayName: (profile && profile.displayName) || 'Chronicler',
      avatarUrl: (profile && profile.avatarUrl) || null,
      level: lvl.level,
      xpIntoLevel: lvl.xpIntoLevel,
      xpForNextLevel: lvl.xpForNextLevel,
      progress01: lvl.progress01,
      pendingCount,
      isFreshlyIncreased,
    };
  }

  // Module-local: last pendingCount we sent to the card. Lets the refresh()
  // loop detect NEW pending events (vs. ambient pending) so the mini-card
  // can fire an extra-attention pulse for freshly-landed things — the
  // "friendly neighbor waving you over" signal. Initialized lazily on the
  // first refresh (to `null` so the first render never false-fires).
  let lastPendingCountSeen = null;

  /**
   * Pure detector for "did pendingCount just rise?". Extracted so the
   * rule is unit-testable without a live IDB or DOM. The three-case
   * truth table:
   *   previous=null, current=anything   → false  (first render baseline)
   *   previous=N,    current<=N         → false  (same or decreased)
   *   previous=N,    current>N          → true   (fresh landing)
   *
   * Called from refresh() as part of the view-model build; the return
   * value is then passed through to mini_card.js which applies the
   * transient .pf-mini-card-fresh class.
   *
   * @param {number|null} previous - last rendered pendingCount, or null
   * @param {number}      current  - just-computed pendingCount
   * @returns {boolean} true only when current strictly exceeds a known previous
   */
  function detectFreshIncrease(previous, current) {
    if (previous === null || previous === undefined) return false;
    return Number(current) > Number(previous);
  }

  /**
   * Compute stats from IDB and push a fresh view model into the card.
   * Never throws — the card always renders something, even if IDB is unreachable.
   */
  async function refresh(card) {
    try {
      const data = await readAllStores();
      const settings = loadSettings();
      // Merge IDB-derived stats with settings-derived prompt stats so
      // achievement criteria can read both from one stat bundle. Also
      // inject counter data so counter-backed achievements can unlock.
      const stats = { ...computeStats(data), ...computePromptStats(settings) };
      try { stats.counters = getCounters(); } catch { stats.counters = {}; }
      // Inject streak state for any streak-based achievement criteria.
      // Note: we do NOT call recordActivityForStreak here — mini-card
      // refreshes fire on every settings-changed event (many per session),
      // and the recordActivity idempotency guard keys on day-rollover.
      // Calling it here would be correct but noisy — cleaner to confine
      // the "activity" signal to explicit user actions (profile open,
      // memory tool open) that already handle it.
      try { stats.streaks = getStreaks(); } catch { stats.streaks = { current: 0, longest: 0 }; }
      // Celebrant achievement criteria read stats.eventsResponded —
      // distinct events the user has completed at least one prompt for.
      // countEventsResponded is in the same IIFE scope (bundled from
      // events/participation.js).
      try {
        stats.eventsResponded = countEventsResponded();
      } catch { stats.eventsResponded = 0; }
      const unlockedIds = computeUnlockedIds(stats);

      // First deploy: treat current unlocks as "already seen" so this commit
      // doesn't pulse for every pre-existing achievement. Subsequent loads
      // only pulse for actually-new ones. Same for prompts — don't pulse just
      // because the feature is new to the user.
      initSeenOnFirstRun(unlockedIds);
      initPromptsOnFirstRun();
      // Record the first-detected unlock date for each unlocked achievement
      // (idempotent; existing entries preserved). Done here so background
      // refreshes capture unlock dates even if the user never opens the
      // full profile page.
      try { recordUnlockDates(unlockedIds); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

      const pendingIds = computePendingAchievements(unlockedIds);
      const cadence = (settings && settings.prompts && settings.prompts.cadence) || 'weekly';
      const newPromptsPending = cadence === 'daily' ? hasNewDayPending() : hasNewWeekPending();
      const activeEventIds = getActiveEventIds();
      const pendingEvents = computePendingEvents(activeEventIds);
      const pendingCount =
        pendingIds.length +
        (newPromptsPending ? 1 : 0) +
        pendingEvents.length;

      // Detect a freshly-landed pending event: on the second+ refresh of
      // the session, if the count STRICTLY increased vs. what we rendered
      // last time, flag it for the mini-card's extra-attention pulse. The
      // null check (in detectFreshIncrease) prevents the very first render
      // from false-firing (e.g. if the user already has 3 pending from
      // last session — we don't want the wave on page load, only when
      // something NEW appears).
      const isFreshlyIncreased = detectFreshIncrease(lastPendingCountSeen, pendingCount);
      lastPendingCountSeen = pendingCount;

      card.update(buildViewModel(
        stats,
        settings && settings.profile,
        pendingCount,
        isFreshlyIncreased,
      ));
    } catch (e) {
      if (!refresh._warned) {
        refresh._warned = true;
        console.warn('[pf] profile refresh failed:', e && e.message);
      }
    }
  }

  /**
   * Start the profile feature. Idempotent — calling twice is a no-op.
   */
  async function start() {
    if (start._started) return;
    start._started = true;

    const card = await mountMiniCard({
      buildElement: () => createMiniCard({
        onOpen: () => {
          openFullPage().catch(e => {
            console.warn('[pf] failed to open profile page:', e && e.message);
          });
        },
      }),
      onMounted: (cardEl) => {
        // Inject the Memory & Lore button directly after the mini-card so
        // it lives in the same sidebar slot. Stop-propagation inside the
        // button prevents click bubbling into the card's open-profile handler.
        const btn = createMemoryButton({
          onClick: () => {
            openMemoryWindow().catch(e => {
              console.warn('[pf] failed to open memory window:', e && e.message);
            });
          },
        });
        cardEl.parentNode.appendChild(btn);
      },
    });

    if (!card) {
      // Sidebar never appeared (unusual — happens if the chat page didn't load).
      // Nothing to update, nothing to clean up.
      return;
    }

    console.info('[pf] profile fork active — mini-card mounted');

    // ---- Chat UX enhancements (Batch 1) ----
    // Message controls: copy, edit, delete, regen buttons on each message.
    // Waits a tick for chatMessagesEl to be in the DOM (it may not exist
    // at the exact moment start() runs if the chat UI initializes async).
    try {
      if (document.getElementById('chatMessagesEl')) {
        initMessageControls();
      } else {
        // Retry after a short delay — chat DOM might still be loading
        setTimeout(() => { try { initMessageControls(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 1500);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Chat search: sidebar thread filter
    try {
      if (document.getElementById('chatThreads')) {
        initChatSearch();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initChatSearch(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 1500);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Stop generating: button + aiTextPlugin monkey-patch
    // (also handles dynamic glossary injection via the same patch)
    try {
      if (window.root && window.root.aiTextPlugin) {
        initStopGenerating();
      } else {
        // root.aiTextPlugin might load async — retry
        setTimeout(() => { try { initStopGenerating(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 3000);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Token count display (Batch 2)
    try {
      if (document.getElementById('chatMessagesEl')) {
        initTokenDisplay();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initTokenDisplay(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 1500);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Chat export (Batch 5) — ⬇ button in chat header
    try { initChatExport(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Thread archiving (Batch 5) — archive buttons on threads
    try {
      if (document.getElementById('chatThreads')) {
        initThreadArchive();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initThreadArchive(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 1500);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Impersonation — now handled by AI Writer (✍ mode picker)
    // Writing enhancer — now handled by AI Writer
    // Narration — now handled by AI Writer

    // Keyboard shortcuts (Batch 6)
    try { initKeyboardShortcuts(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Prompt presets (Batch 6) — 📋 dropdown near input
    try { initPromptPresets(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Quick reminder editor (Batch 6) — 📌 button near input
    // Quick reminder — now handled by Context Editor

    // Fullscreen toggle (Batch 7) — ⛶ button in header
    try { initFullscreen(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Bulk thread operations (Batch 5) — multi-select in sidebar
    try {
      if (document.getElementById('chatThreads')) {
        initBulkThreads();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initBulkThreads(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 1500);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // AI reasoning toggle (Batch 6) — 🧠/💭 in header
    try { initReasoningToggle(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Font settings (Batch 7) — Aa dropdown in header
    try { initFontSettings(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Generation settings (Batch 6) — ⚙ temp/tokens near input
    try { initGenSettings(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Image generation (Batch 4) — 🖼 button on AI messages
    try {
      if (document.getElementById('chatMessagesEl') && window.root && window.root.textToImagePlugin) {
        initImageGen();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initImageGen(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 3000);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Theme toggle (Batch 7) — ☀/🌙 in header
    try { initThemeToggle(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Custom background (Batch 7) — 🏞 in header
    try { initCustomBg(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Auto-summary (Batch 2) — compress older messages
    try {
      if (document.getElementById('chatMessagesEl')) {
        initAutoSummary();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initAutoSummary(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 1500);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Character browser (Batch 5) — 👥 in header
    try {
      if (window.db && window.db.characters) {
        initCharBrowser();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initCharBrowser(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 3000);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // UI animation polish + mobile refinements (Batch 7)
    try { initUiPolish(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Conversation branching (Batch 6)
    try {
      if (document.getElementById('chatMessagesEl')) {
        initBranching();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initBranching(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 1500);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Document analysis (Batch 6) — 📎 upload text files
    try { initDocAnalysis(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Anti-repetition — UI superseded by Context Editor banlist tab;
    // buildAntiRepetitionBlock() still contributes to the injection pipeline.

    // Dice roller (Batch 8) — 🎲 button + /roll command
    try { initDiceRoller(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Message timestamps (Batch 8)
    try {
      if (document.getElementById('chatMessagesEl')) {
        initTimestamps();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initTimestamps(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 1500);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Auto-lorebook (Batch 8) — 🔮 AI generates glossary entries
    try {
      if (window.root && window.root.aiTextPlugin) {
        initAutoLorebook();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initAutoLorebook(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 3000);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // User persona — UI superseded by Context Editor persona tab;
    // buildPersonaBlock() still contributes to the injection pipeline.

    // Character card import/export (Batch 9) — 🃏 in header
    try {
      if (window.db && window.db.characters) {
        initCharCards();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initCharCards(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 3000);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Code syntax highlighting (Batch 3)
    try {
      if (document.getElementById('chatMessagesEl')) {
        initCodeHighlight();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initCodeHighlight(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 1500);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Voice I/O (Batch 3) — mic + speaker buttons
    try { initVoice(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Combined tools — unified entry points for grouped features
    // AI Writer: impersonate + narrate + enhance + recap in one dropdown
    try {
      if (window.root && window.root.aiTextPlugin) {
        initAiWriter();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initAiWriter(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 3000);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Context Editor: glossary + banlist + reminder + persona in tabbed modal
    try { initContextEditor(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Tools menu (MUST be last — collects all buttons injected above)
    try { initToolsMenu(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Context dashboard (shows what's injected into AI prompt)
    try { initContextDashboard(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Recap — superseded by AI Writer's 📜 Recap mode.

    // Message bookmarks — star important messages
    try {
      if (document.getElementById('chatMessagesEl')) {
        initBookmarks();
      } else {
        setTimeout(() => { try { initBookmarks(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 1500);
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Daily quest — AI-generated date-seeded quest card
    try {
      setTimeout(() => { try { initDailyQuest(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ } }, 2000);
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Initial fetch
    await refresh(card);

    // Boot-time app-wide accent paint. Cascades the user's picked
    // accent into upstream Perchance's CSS variables so the whole app
    // re-tints BEFORE the user even opens the profile. Safe no-op if
    // the user hasn't picked an accent (resolves to amber, which is
    // close to the default #005ac2 blue upstream ships with — still
    // a noticeable shift, but that's the user's point: "even the
    // standard Perchance has been replaced by the new theme color").
    //
    // Derived from the same settings+stats+unlocked signal the
    // profile uses — so if the user picks an accent they haven't
    // unlocked yet (e.g. from a restored backup), we fall back to
    // amber consistently everywhere.
    try {
      const bootSettings = loadSettings();
      const bootStats = await (async () => {
        try {
          const data = await readAllStores();
          return { ...computeStats(data), ...computePromptStats(bootSettings) };
        } catch { return {}; }
      })();
      const bootUnlocked = computeUnlockedIds(bootStats);
      // Two-pass: inject _unlockedCount so palette threshold achievements
      // (palette_vellum, palette_silver, palette_deep) fire at boot.
      // Without this, the user's chosen vellum/silver color would flash
      // to default on page load until the profile overlay re-resolves.
      bootStats._unlockedCount = bootUnlocked.length;
      try { bootStats.counters = getCounters(); } catch { bootStats.counters = {}; }
      const bootUnlockedFull = computeUnlockedIds(bootStats);
      paintAllChannels(bootSettings, bootStats, bootUnlockedFull);
    } catch { /* non-fatal — upstream theming is a nicety, not a promise */ }

    // ---- Theme colors (custom background gradient) ----
    // Apply user's chosen primary/secondary background colors at boot.
    // applyThemeColorsLive() is in the same IIFE scope (details_form.js).
    try {
      if (typeof applyThemeColorsLive === 'function') {
        applyThemeColorsLive();
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // ---- Share-link detection ----
    // If the URL contains ?h=<shareCode>, auto-open the card viewer
    // overlay showing the shared profile. openShareViewer is in the
    // same IIFE scope (bundled from render/share_viewer.js).
    try {
      const sharedVM = parseShareUrl();
      if (sharedVM) {
        try { openShareViewer(sharedVM); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
      }
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    // Live refresh when the user changes settings (avatar, title, etc.) —
    // no need to wait for the 30s interval.
    onSettingsChange(() => { refresh(card); });

    // Periodic refresh. Cheap — IDB getAll is fast at Perchance scale,
    // and we don't hook into message events, keeping the surface tiny.
    //
    // We hold onto the interval handle so it can be cleared on page
    // unload (belt + suspenders against browsers that keep timers
    // alive across bfcache navigations) AND we defensively skip the
    // refresh if the card element has been removed from the DOM for
    // any reason — the interval self-clears in that case so we don't
    // keep doing IDB reads against a ghost node.
    const refreshHandle = setInterval(() => {
      if (!card.isConnected) {
        clearInterval(refreshHandle);
        return;
      }
      refresh(card);
    }, REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS);

    // Page unload / bfcache — clear the interval. `pagehide` fires in
    // more cases than `beforeunload` (bfcache, mobile background, etc.)
    // so it's the more defensive choice. One-shot; no teardown needed.
    const onPageHide = () => { clearInterval(refreshHandle); };
    window.addEventListener('pagehide', onPageHide, { once: true });

    // Refresh when the tab becomes visible again — catches "user came back
    // after a while" without waiting for the next interval tick.
    document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', () => {
      if (document.visibilityState === 'visible') refresh(card);
    });
  }

  /* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     module: chat/character_favicon.js
     ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  // chat/character_favicon.js
  //
  // Adapted from HNE's session-favicon swap (perchance_2.txt around line
  // 20776 in the HNE source). HNE swaps the browser-tab favicon to the
  // user's "personal symbol" while a hypnosis session is playing.
  //
  // Memory Hero adapts this to chat: while a thread is open, the active
  // CHARACTER's avatar becomes the tab favicon. Reverts on thread close
  // or page navigation. Pure visual polish — gives each chat tab visual
  // identity matching whomever you're talking to, useful when a user has
  // many tabs open across different characters.
  //
  // Strategy: poll the chat messages container every ~1.5s and read the
  // first character avatar (img tag with class .messageAvatar or similar)
  // out of the rendered DOM. Robust against upstream refactors because
  // we don't reach into private state — we observe rendered output.
  //
  // Falls back to no-op if root.faviconPlugin isn't loaded; still works
  // by directly setting <link rel="icon">.href in that case.
  //
  // Bootstrap: idempotent. Self-installs after DOM ready.

  function initCharacterFavicon() {
    if (initCharacterFavicon._done) return;
    initCharacterFavicon._done = true;

    let _originalFaviconHref = null;
    let _lastAppliedAvatarUrl = null;

    function getOrCreateFaviconLink() {
      let link = document.querySelector('link[rel~="icon"]');
      if (!link) {
        link = document.createElement('link');
        link.rel = 'icon';
        document.head.appendChild(link);
      }
      return link;
    }

    function captureOriginal() {
      const link = getOrCreateFaviconLink();
      if (_originalFaviconHref == null) _originalFaviconHref = link.href || '';
    }

    function setFavicon(url) {
      if (!url || url === _lastAppliedAvatarUrl) return;
      captureOriginal();
      const link = getOrCreateFaviconLink();
      link.href = url;
      _lastAppliedAvatarUrl = url;
      // Prefer the favicon-plugin if it loaded (handles cross-browser
      // quirks better than direct DOM mutation). Safe no-op if absent.
      try {
        if (typeof window.root !== 'undefined' && window.root.faviconPlugin?.set) {
          window.root.faviconPlugin.set(url);
        }
      } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
    }

    function revertFavicon() {
      if (_originalFaviconHref == null || _lastAppliedAvatarUrl == null) return;
      const link = getOrCreateFaviconLink();
      link.href = _originalFaviconHref;
      _lastAppliedAvatarUrl = null;
      try {
        if (typeof window.root !== 'undefined' && window.root.faviconPlugin?.set
            && _originalFaviconHref) {
          window.root.faviconPlugin.set(_originalFaviconHref);
        }
      } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
    }

    // Find the active character avatar by looking at the chat messages
    // panel. Upstream renders character avatar inside .characterAvatar /
    // [data-avatar-url] / <img> in messages. We scan defensively.
    function findActiveCharacterAvatar() {
      try {
        // Try several known upstream selectors first; bail on the first hit.
        const selectors = [
          '#chatMessagesEl .messageAvatar img[src]',
          '#chatMessagesEl img.characterAvatar[src]',
          '.chat-area .characterAvatar img[src]',
          '.thread-header img.avatar[src]',
          '[data-character-avatar][src]',
        ];
        for (const sel of selectors) {
          const el = document.querySelector(sel);
          const url = el && (el.getAttribute('src') || el.dataset.characterAvatar);
          if (url && /^https?:|^data:/.test(url)) return url;
        }
        return null;
      } catch {
        return null;
      }
    }

    function tick() {
      try {
        // Don't fight the user when the tab isn't active — they can't see
        // the favicon anyway, and pausing here saves work.
        if (document.hidden) return;
        const avatarUrl = findActiveCharacterAvatar();
        if (avatarUrl) setFavicon(avatarUrl);
        else if (_lastAppliedAvatarUrl) revertFavicon();
      } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
    }

    // 1.5s tick — fast enough that switching threads feels instant, slow
    // enough not to thrash. Pause when document.hidden via the early
    // return inside tick().
    setInterval(tick, 1500);
    // Run once on init (after a short delay so upstream has rendered).
    setTimeout(tick, 800);
  }

  /* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     module: chat/fresh_anchors.js
     ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  // chat/fresh_anchors.js
  //
  // Adapted from HNE's freshSeeds rotation memory (perchance_2.txt around
  // its buildFreshnessBlock pattern). The corpus itself lives in
  // memory_hero_1.txt as a top-level Perchance function (so other
  // generators can import it via root.memHero.freshSeeds).
  //
  // This module exposes a small in-app helper that:
  //   1. Maintains a session-scoped "recently used" rotation memory
  //      (last ~8 phrases) so the same anchor doesn't appear back-to-back.
  //   2. Calls root.freshSeeds(...) when available, with the rotation
  //      memory passed as excludeSet.
  //   3. Returns a short string suitable for splicing into AI prompt
  //      assembly OR returns "" if the plugin isn't loaded (safe no-op).
  //
  // The chat code can call window.__memHero_freshAnchors(profileTags, n)
  // to get a freshly-shuffled chunk of anchor phrases. By default the
  // chat path does NOT auto-inject — wiring this into the prompt builder
  // is opt-in, deliberately, so users keep control of what reaches the
  // model. This module just makes the helper available.
  //
  // Bootstrap: registers the helper on window. Idempotent.

  function initFreshAnchors() {
    if (initFreshAnchors._done) return;
    initFreshAnchors._done = true;

    // Rotation memory — phrase strings most recently returned, oldest first.
    // Capped at ROTATION_CAP so the exclude set doesn't grow forever.
    const ROTATION_CAP = 8;
    const _recent = [];

    function _push(phrase) {
      if (!phrase) return;
      // De-dupe: if already recent, move to most-recent slot rather than dup.
      const idx = _recent.indexOf(phrase);
      if (idx >= 0) _recent.splice(idx, 1);
      _recent.push(phrase);
      while (_recent.length > ROTATION_CAP) _recent.shift();
    }

    /**
     * Get up to n fresh anchor phrases tagged for the given profile/scene.
     * Returns [] if the freshSeeds plugin isn't reachable.
     *
     * @param {string[]} profileTags  any-match tag filter (e.g. ['mystery'])
     * @param {number}   n            how many phrases to return (default 3)
     * @returns {string[]}
     */
    function freshAnchors(profileTags, n) {
      try {
        const r = (typeof window !== 'undefined') ? window.root : null;
        if (!r || typeof r.freshSeeds !== 'function') return [];
        const arr = r.freshSeeds({
          profileTags: Array.isArray(profileTags) ? profileTags : [],
          n: Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0 ? n : 3,
          excludeSet: _recent.slice(),
        });
        if (!Array.isArray(arr)) return [];
        for (const p of arr) _push(p);
        return arr;
      } catch {
        return [];
      }
    }

    /**
     * Convenience: format anchors as a single inline prompt fragment.
     * Empty string when nothing's available, so callers can `+` it.
     */
    function freshAnchorsFragment(profileTags, n) {
      const arr = freshAnchors(profileTags, n);
      if (!arr.length) return '';
      return 'Optional sensory imagery you may weave in if it fits: '
           + arr.map(p => `"${p}"`).join('; ') + '.';
    }

    // Expose globally so chat prompt assembly (or custom code) can opt in.
    if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
      window.__memHero_freshAnchors = freshAnchors;
      window.__memHero_freshAnchorsFragment = freshAnchorsFragment;
    }
  }

  /* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     module: chat/community_packs.js
     ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  // chat/community_packs.js
  //
  // Adapted from HNE's community-pack loader (perchance_1.txt
  // communityPacks list + lazy fetch in HNE's persona browser).
  //
  // Memory Hero pulls in third-party Perchance generators that expose a
  // `characters` list (or `lore`, `prompts`). Each pack is registered in
  // memory_hero_1.txt under `communityPacks` via dynamicImport(). We DON'T
  // touch them at boot — instead this module exposes a one-shot loader
  // that the character browser / lore picker / quest UI can call when
  // the user opens those surfaces.
  //
  // Two stable shapes are returned by loadCommunityPacks():
  //   {
  //     characters: [ {id, name, roleInstruction, avatarUrl?, ...}, ...],
  //     lore:       [ {text, ...}, ...],
  //     prompts:    [ {text, category?, ...}, ...],
  //     packMeta:   [ {packKey, characterCount, loreCount, promptCount}, ...]
  //   }
  //
  // Each top-level array merges entries across all registered packs in
  // declaration order. Pack metadata is preserved so the UI can show
  // "from <packName>" attributions.
  //
  // Bootstrap: registers loadCommunityPacks on window. Idempotent.

  function initCommunityPacks() {
    if (initCommunityPacks._done) return;
    initCommunityPacks._done = true;

    let _cached = null;
    let _inFlight = null;

    /**
     * Lazy fetch all registered community packs and return a merged
     * {characters, lore, prompts, packMeta} bundle. Cached across calls.
     * Awaiting concurrent calls de-duplicates the work.
     *
     * @returns {Promise<{characters:Array, lore:Array, prompts:Array, packMeta:Array}>}
     */
    async function loadCommunityPacks() {
      if (_cached) return _cached;
      if (_inFlight) return _inFlight;

      _inFlight = (async () => {
        const merged = { characters: [], lore: [], prompts: [], packMeta: [] };
        try {
          const r = (typeof window !== 'undefined') ? window.root : null;
          const cp = r && r.communityPacks;
          if (!cp || typeof cp !== 'object') return merged;

          // Pack key → list of entries. Each pack is the result of
          // dynamicImport, which exposes any top-level lists from the
          // remote generator. We read the three known shapes.
          for (const packKey of Object.keys(cp)) {
            try {
              // dynamicImport returns either a Perchance list-array or
              // an object containing several lists; both shapes are
              // valid. Accommodate both.
              const pack = cp[packKey];
              if (!pack) continue;

              // Normalize "list of strings or list of JSON" — Perchance
              // lists often surface as arrays whose `.toString()` returns
              // a random pick. We want the underlying array.
              const characters = _readPackList(pack, 'characters');
              const lore       = _readPackList(pack, 'lore');
              const prompts    = _readPackList(pack, 'prompts');

              for (const c of characters) merged.characters.push({ ...c, _packKey: packKey });
              for (const l of lore)       merged.lore.push({ ...l, _packKey: packKey });
              for (const p of prompts)    merged.prompts.push({ ...p, _packKey: packKey });

              merged.packMeta.push({
                packKey,
                characterCount: characters.length,
                loreCount: lore.length,
                promptCount: prompts.length,
              });
            } catch (e) {
              // One pack failing shouldn't kill the rest. Surface to
              // console for the pack author and move on.
              console.warn(`[memHero] community pack '${packKey}' failed to load:`, e);
            }
          }
        } catch (e) {
          console.warn('[memHero] loadCommunityPacks failed:', e);
        }
        _cached = merged;
        return merged;
      })();

      const result = await _inFlight;
      _inFlight = null;
      return result;
    }

    /** Read one named list out of a dynamicImport pack object. */
    function _readPackList(pack, listName) {
      try {
        const list = pack && pack[listName];
        if (!list) return [];
        // Array shape: list.length, list[i] is JSON-string or object.
        if (Array.isArray(list)) {
          return list.map(_coerceEntry).filter(Boolean);
        }
        // Perchance list shape: has __ITEMS or numeric-keyed entries.
        if (typeof list === 'object') {
          const keys = Object.keys(list).filter(k => /^\d+$/.test(k));
          if (keys.length) {
            return keys.map(k => _coerceEntry(list[k])).filter(Boolean);
          }
        }
        return [];
      } catch {
        return [];
      }
    }

    /** A pack entry might be a JSON string, an object, or a plain phrase. */
    function _coerceEntry(raw) {
      if (raw == null) return null;
      if (typeof raw === 'string') {
        const trimmed = raw.trim();
        if (!trimmed) return null;
        // Try JSON parse first — pack authors typically put JSON objects.
        if (trimmed.startsWith('{') || trimmed.startsWith('[')) {
          try { return JSON.parse(trimmed); } catch { /* fall through */ }
        }
        // Plain phrase (e.g. a lore line).
        return { text: trimmed };
      }
      if (typeof raw === 'object') return raw;
      return null;
    }

    /** Throw away the cache so the next call re-reads the registered packs. */
    function invalidateCommunityPacks() {
      _cached = null;
      _inFlight = null;
    }

    // Expose globally so character browser / lore picker / quest UI can use it.
    if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
      window.__memHero_loadCommunityPacks = loadCommunityPacks;
      window.__memHero_invalidateCommunityPacks = invalidateCommunityPacks;
    }
  }

  /* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     module: chat/bg_slideshow.js
     ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  // chat/bg_slideshow.js
  //
  // Live AI-generated chat background slideshow with a soft Ken Burns
  // effect. Adapted from AI Slideshow (perchance.org/ai-slideshow):
  //   - dual-slide crossfade pattern (slideA / slideB)
  //   - Ken Burns transition (slow scale + translate over the dwell interval)
  //   - root.textToImagePlugin's strict {prompt, negative} contract
  //
  // Differences from AI Slideshow:
  //   - Subliminal sizing: 6% zoom + ~1.5% pan (vs. AI Slideshow's 18%
  //     foreground sizing). The slideshow lives BEHIND chat content, so
  //     motion has to be calm enough not to compete with reading.
  //   - Body-level fixed positioning. Mounting inside #chatMessagesEl
  //     would make the bg scroll with messages, which we don't want.
  //     A fixed container at body level is robust to upstream DOM
  //     refactors and to the chat element's overflow:auto.
  //   - Tight memory cap (8 images max, top-up to 4) — chat is a
  //     long-lived page, we can't accumulate forever.
  //   - In-memory only — no IndexedDB persistence (intentional: keeps
  //     storage budget for chat history; the slideshow regenerates on
  //     reload, which is cheap with cached prompts).
  //
  // Storage: settings.chatBgSlideshow = { enabled, prompt, intervalSec, opacity }
  //
  // Bootstrap: call initBgSlideshow() from start() OR self-bootstrap below.
  // Idempotent.

  function initBgSlideshow() {
    if (initBgSlideshow._done) return;
    initBgSlideshow._done = true;

    // ---- Tunables ----
    const MAX_IMAGES         = 8;     // hard cap — protects browser memory
    const TOPUP_TARGET       = 4;     // background top-up aims for this many cached
    const DEFAULT_INTERVAL   = 12;    // seconds per slide (dwell)
    const DEFAULT_FADE_SEC   = 1.8;   // crossfade duration
    const DEFAULT_OPACITY    = 0.35;  // dim so chat stays readable
    const DEFAULT_PROMPT     = 'soft cinematic atmosphere, dreamy color palette, '
                             + 'ethereal lighting, abstract scene, painterly';
    const DEFAULT_NEGATIVE   = 'text, watermark, logo, deformed, distorted, low quality, '
                             + 'blurry artifacts, signature, ui elements';

    // Subtle Ken Burns paths — pick one at random per Ken-Burns advance.
    // Each path is reverse-symmetric to its counterpart so successive
    // slides don't drift in the same direction. Numbers tuned to be
    // just-noticeable (you read "the room is breathing") without being
    // distracting.
    const KEN_BURNS_PATHS = [
      { from: 'scale(1.00) translate(0, 0)',          to: 'scale(1.06) translate(-1.5%, -0.8%)' },
      { from: 'scale(1.06) translate(-1.2%, -1.0%)',  to: 'scale(1.00) translate(0, 0)'        },
      { from: 'scale(1.00) translate(0, 0)',          to: 'scale(1.06) translate(1.5%,  -0.6%)' },
      { from: 'scale(1.05) translate(1.0%, 1.0%)',    to: 'scale(1.00) translate(0, 0)'        },
      { from: 'scale(1.00) translate(0, 0)',          to: 'scale(1.05) translate(0,    -1.2%)' },
      { from: 'scale(1.05) translate(0,  1.0%)',      to: 'scale(1.00) translate(0, 0)'        },
    ];

    // Transition catalog — Ken Burns is the workhorse (weighted heavily),
    // others add subtle variety. All five are designed to stay UNDER the
    // chat content, so motion is calm and short. Adapted from AI Slideshow's
    // larger transition library (perchance.org/ai-slideshow), but every
    // one has been redesigned for backdrop use:
    //   - smaller transforms so text doesn't appear to "wobble" against motion
    //   - no 100%-translate effects (would briefly hide entire image)
    //   - no fade-to-black/white flashes (would startle a reader)
    //
    // weight = relative pick probability (sums don't need to be 1).
    // Ken Burns gets weight 5 vs. 1-2 each for variety transitions, so
    // ~60% of advances are Ken Burns and the rest mix things up.
    const TRANSITIONS = {
      kenBurns: {
        label:  'Ken Burns',
        weight: 5,
        run(oldEl, newEl, url, fadeSec, intervalSec) {
          const z = KEN_BURNS_PATHS[Math.floor(Math.random() * KEN_BURNS_PATHS.length)];
          newEl.style.transition = 'none';
          newEl.style.opacity = '0';
          newEl.style.transform = z.from;
          newEl.style.filter = '';
          newEl.style.backgroundImage = `url("${url}")`;
          void newEl.offsetWidth;
          newEl.style.transition =
            `opacity ${fadeSec}s ease, transform ${intervalSec}s ease-in-out`;
          newEl.style.opacity = String(state.opacity);
          newEl.style.transform = z.to;
          newEl.style.zIndex = '2';
          if (oldEl) {
            oldEl.style.transition = `opacity ${fadeSec}s ease`;
            oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
            oldEl.style.zIndex = '1';
          }
        },
      },
      crossfade: {
        // Pure crossfade — no motion. A breath between Ken Burns picks.
        label:  'Crossfade',
        weight: 2,
        run(oldEl, newEl, url, fadeSec /*, intervalSec*/) {
          newEl.style.transition = 'none';
          newEl.style.opacity = '0';
          newEl.style.transform = 'scale(1.00)';
          newEl.style.filter = '';
          newEl.style.backgroundImage = `url("${url}")`;
          void newEl.offsetWidth;
          newEl.style.transition = `opacity ${fadeSec}s ease`;
          newEl.style.opacity = String(state.opacity);
          newEl.style.zIndex = '2';
          if (oldEl) {
            oldEl.style.transition = `opacity ${fadeSec}s ease`;
            oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
            oldEl.style.zIndex = '1';
          }
        },
      },
      slowZoom: {
        // Slow continuous zoom-in — no panning, just a steady push.
        // Calmer than Ken Burns but never static.
        label:  'Slow zoom',
        weight: 2,
        run(oldEl, newEl, url, fadeSec, intervalSec) {
          newEl.style.transition = 'none';
          newEl.style.opacity = '0';
          newEl.style.transform = 'scale(1.00)';
          newEl.style.filter = '';
          newEl.style.backgroundImage = `url("${url}")`;
          void newEl.offsetWidth;
          newEl.style.transition =
            `opacity ${fadeSec}s ease, transform ${intervalSec}s linear`;
          newEl.style.opacity = String(state.opacity);
          newEl.style.transform = 'scale(1.07)';
          newEl.style.zIndex = '2';
          if (oldEl) {
            oldEl.style.transition = `opacity ${fadeSec}s ease`;
            oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
            oldEl.style.zIndex = '1';
          }
        },
      },
      blurFade: {
        // Crossfade with a brief blur on entry — gives a dreamlike
        // "settling into focus" feel. Total blur travel is short
        // (10px → 0) so it doesn't feel mushy.
        label:  'Blur fade',
        weight: 1,
        run(oldEl, newEl, url, fadeSec /*, intervalSec*/) {
          newEl.style.transition = 'none';
          newEl.style.opacity = '0';
          newEl.style.transform = 'scale(1.02)';
          newEl.style.filter = 'blur(10px)';
          newEl.style.backgroundImage = `url("${url}")`;
          void newEl.offsetWidth;
          newEl.style.transition =
            `opacity ${fadeSec}s ease, filter ${fadeSec}s ease, transform ${fadeSec}s ease`;
          newEl.style.opacity = String(state.opacity);
          newEl.style.filter = 'blur(0px)';
          newEl.style.transform = 'scale(1.00)';
          newEl.style.zIndex = '2';
          if (oldEl) {
            oldEl.style.transition = `opacity ${fadeSec}s ease, filter ${fadeSec}s ease`;
            oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
            oldEl.style.filter = 'blur(6px)';
            oldEl.style.zIndex = '1';
          }
        },
      },
      parallaxPan: {
        // Diagonal pan only — no zoom. Like a slow truck shot. Direction
        // alternates so successive panPerformanceparallax picks alternate left/right.
        label:  'Parallax pan',
        weight: 1,
        _dir: 1, // mutable: flips each call
        run(oldEl, newEl, url, fadeSec, intervalSec) {
          this._dir = -this._dir;
          const dir = this._dir;
          newEl.style.transition = 'none';
          newEl.style.opacity = '0';
          newEl.style.transform = `scale(1.04) translate(${-1.8 * dir}%, 0)`;
          newEl.style.filter = '';
          newEl.style.backgroundImage = `url("${url}")`;
          void newEl.offsetWidth;
          newEl.style.transition =
            `opacity ${fadeSec}s ease, transform ${intervalSec}s ease-in-out`;
          newEl.style.opacity = String(state.opacity);
          newEl.style.transform = `scale(1.04) translate(${1.8 * dir}%, 0)`;
          newEl.style.zIndex = '2';
          if (oldEl) {
            oldEl.style.transition = `opacity ${fadeSec}s ease`;
            oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
            oldEl.style.zIndex = '1';
          }
        },
      },
    };

    // Build a flat picker array honoring weights — pre-expanded so the
    // hot path is just a single array index, no on-the-fly weight math.
    const TRANSITION_PICKER = (() => {
      const arr = [];
      for (const [id, t] of Object.entries(TRANSITIONS)) {
        for (let i = 0; i < (t.weight || 1); i++) arr.push(id);
      }
      return arr;
    })();

    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Style preset chips — adapted from AI Slideshow's STYLE_PRESETS.
    // Click a chip to APPEND its prompt fragment to the user's prompt;
    // click again to remove. Negatives merge in the same way. Active
    // chips persist across reloads via state.activeStyleIds.
    // Reduced from AI Slideshow's 19 presets to 12 chat-friendly ones
    // (atmosphere + nature + style only — no abstract category, since
    // figurative imagery reads more "scene" than "screensaver").
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    const STYLE_PRESETS = [
      // Atmosphere
      { id: 'misty',      label: 'Misty',         prompt: 'misty fog, drifting low clouds, ethereal haze, soft visibility' },
      { id: 'twilight',   label: 'Twilight',      prompt: 'twilight blue hour, indigo and violet sky, soft fading light' },
      { id: 'aurora',     label: 'Aurora',        prompt: 'aurora borealis ribbons, soft green and violet light bands, starry night' },
      { id: 'cosmic',     label: 'Cosmic',        prompt: 'deep space nebula, soft star clusters, swirling cosmic dust, indigo and magenta' },
      // Nature
      { id: 'forest',     label: 'Forest',        prompt: 'ancient forest at dusk, soft mossy ground, dappled fading light, deep shadows' },
      { id: 'ocean',      label: 'Ocean',         prompt: 'calm ocean horizon, slow gentle waves, soft pastel sky, infinite distance' },
      { id: 'mountains',  label: 'Mountains',     prompt: 'distant snow-capped mountains, soft layered haze, muted blue-gray palette' },
      { id: 'meadow',     label: 'Meadow',        prompt: 'soft grass meadow at dawn, golden mist, warm muted tones' },
      // Style
      { id: 'watercolor', label: 'Watercolor',    prompt: 'soft watercolor wash, gentle color bleeds, subtle paper texture, hand-painted' },
      { id: 'oil',        label: 'Oil paint',     prompt: 'soft oil painting, romantic style, visible brush strokes, muted impressionist palette' },
      { id: 'film',       label: 'Film',          prompt: 'analog film photograph, soft grain, faded vintage palette, warm muted highlights' },
      { id: 'longexp',    label: 'Long exposure', prompt: 'long exposure photograph, soft motion blur trails, smooth water and clouds, dreamlike' },
    ];
    const STYLE_PRESET_MAP = Object.fromEntries(STYLE_PRESETS.map(p => [p.id, p]));

    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Prompt enhancer — uses aiTextPlugin to rewrite a casual idea into
    // an optimized AI image generation prompt. Streams back into the
    // textarea. Click the same button while running to cancel.
    //
    // Adapted verbatim from AI Slideshow's ENHANCE_INSTRUCTIONS (which
    // were in turn adapted from HNE's BG_ENHANCE_INSTRUCTIONS, originally
    // from promptyx). The instructions are intentionally strict about
    // format ("ONLY the final raw prompt string") so the streamed result
    // drops straight into the textarea unedited.
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    const ENHANCE_INSTRUCTIONS = {
      short: 'ROLE: Expert AI image prompt engineer.\n'
        + 'ACTION: Transform the input into a concise, optimized AI image generation prompt (300-600 chars).\n'
        + 'FORMAT: Comma-separated precise visual descriptors only.\n'
        + 'ENHANCE WITH: specific lighting type, camera angle/lens, primary color palette, key material/texture detail, mood/atmosphere word, one compositional technique.\n'
        + 'FORBIDDEN: 8K, UHD, hyperrealism, "extreme detail", explanatory sentences, question marks, quotes around the result.\n'
        + 'OUTPUT: ONLY the final raw prompt string. No preamble. No quotes.',
      medium: 'ROLE: Expert AI image prompt engineer.\n'
        + 'ACTION: Transform the input into a richly-detailed AI image generation prompt (600-1100 chars).\n'
        + 'FORMAT: Comma-separated visual descriptors with moderate depth.\n'
        + 'ENHANCE WITH: specific lighting setup, focal length/camera type, 2-3 color palette descriptors, multiple surface textures, spatial composition (foreground/midground/background), atmosphere/weather, time of day, style anchor (one artist or aesthetic reference).\n'
        + 'FORBIDDEN: 8K, UHD, hyperrealism, "extreme detail", narration, instructional language, quotes around the result.\n'
        + 'OUTPUT: ONLY the final raw prompt string. No preamble. No quotes.',
    };
    let _enhanceToken = 0;
    let _enhanceRunning = false;

    // ---- State ----
    const state = {
      enabled:        false,
      prompt:         DEFAULT_PROMPT,
      intervalSec:    DEFAULT_INTERVAL,
      opacity:        DEFAULT_OPACITY,
      activeStyleIds: [],           // STYLE_PRESET ids currently appended to the prompt
      images:         [],           // [{ dataUrl, prompt }]
      cursor:         0,
      slot:          0,             // which slide (0=A, 1=B) is currently shown
      generating:    false,
      advanceTimer:  null,
      container:     null,
      slides:        [null, null],  // [slideA, slideB] DOM refs
    };

    // ---- Boot: load persisted settings ----
    try {
      const s = loadSettings();
      const cfg = (s && s.chatBgSlideshow) || {};
      state.enabled     = !!cfg.enabled;
      state.prompt      = (typeof cfg.prompt === 'string' && cfg.prompt.trim())
                            ? cfg.prompt.trim() : DEFAULT_PROMPT;
      state.intervalSec = Number.isFinite(cfg.intervalSec) && cfg.intervalSec >= 6
                            ? cfg.intervalSec : DEFAULT_INTERVAL;
      state.opacity     = Number.isFinite(cfg.opacity) && cfg.opacity > 0 && cfg.opacity <= 1
                            ? cfg.opacity : DEFAULT_OPACITY;
      // Filter out any preset ids the user might have saved before we
      // rename / delete a preset — keeps the loader robust to migrations.
      state.activeStyleIds = Array.isArray(cfg.activeStyleIds)
                              ? cfg.activeStyleIds.filter(id => STYLE_PRESET_MAP[id])
                              : [];
    } catch { /* non-fatal */ }

    injectCSS();
    buildUI();

    // If already enabled (returning user), spin up.
    if (state.enabled) startSlideshow();

    // ----------------------------------------------------------------
    // Lifecycle
    // ----------------------------------------------------------------

    function startSlideshow() {
      ensureContainer();
      // No images yet → kick off generation; the first one shown wins.
      if (state.images.length === 0) {
        generateMore(2);
      } else {
        state.cursor = 0;
        state.slot = 0;
        showCurrent();
        scheduleNext();
        queueTopUp();
      }
    }

    function stopSlideshow() {
      clearTimeout(state.advanceTimer);
      state.advanceTimer = null;
      if (state.container) {
        state.container.remove();
        state.container = null;
        state.slides = [null, null];
      }
      // Don't clear state.images — keeping them lets re-enabling resume
      // instantly without paying generation cost again.
    }

    function ensureContainer() {
      if (state.container) return;
      const container = document.createElement('div');
      container.className = 'pf-bgss-container';
      const slideA = document.createElement('div');
      slideA.className = 'pf-bgss-slide';
      const slideB = document.createElement('div');
      slideB.className = 'pf-bgss-slide';
      const overlay = document.createElement('div');
      overlay.className = 'pf-bgss-overlay';
      container.appendChild(slideA);
      container.appendChild(slideB);
      container.appendChild(overlay);
      // body-level mount so the slideshow doesn't scroll with chat
      document.body.appendChild(container);
      state.container = container;
      state.slides = [slideA, slideB];
    }

    // ----------------------------------------------------------------
    // Generation — strict {prompt, negative} contract per AI Slideshow's note
    // ----------------------------------------------------------------

    async function generateMore(n) {
      if (state.generating) return;
      state.generating = true;
      try {
        for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
          if (!state.enabled) break;
          if (state.images.length >= MAX_IMAGES) break;
          const dataUrl = await generateOneImage();
          if (dataUrl) {
            state.images.push({ dataUrl, prompt: state.prompt });
            // First image arrived: kick off display
            if (state.images.length === 1 && state.enabled && state.container) {
              state.cursor = 0;
              state.slot = 0;
              showCurrent();
              scheduleNext();
            }
          } else {
            // Backoff if generation failed — don't hammer the plugin
            await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1500));
          }
        }
      } finally {
        state.generating = false;
      }
    }

    /**
     * Build the effective prompt by appending active style preset
     * fragments to the base prompt. Comma-separated, no duplicates.
     */
    function composedPrompt() {
      const base = (state.prompt || DEFAULT_PROMPT).trim();
      const stylePieces = (state.activeStyleIds || [])
        .map(id => STYLE_PRESET_MAP[id])
        .filter(Boolean)
        .map(p => p.prompt);
      if (!stylePieces.length) return base;
      return base + ', ' + stylePieces.join(', ');
    }

    async function generateOneImage() {
      try {
        const r = (typeof window !== 'undefined') ? window.root : null;
        if (!r || typeof r.textToImagePlugin !== 'function') return null;
        // Strict contract: ONLY {prompt, negative}. Extra fields route
        // values through Perchance's DSL evaluator, which throws on
        // brace-containing user input. AI Slideshow's exact warning.
        const result = await r.textToImagePlugin({
          prompt:   composedPrompt(),
          negative: DEFAULT_NEGATIVE,
        });
        const dataUrl = (result && result.dataUrl) || (typeof result === 'string' ? result : null);
        if (typeof dataUrl !== 'string' || !dataUrl.startsWith('data:')) return null;
        return dataUrl;
      } catch (e) {
        console.warn('[memHero bgSlideshow] generation failed:', e && e.message);
        return null;
      }
    }

    function queueTopUp() {
      if (!state.enabled) return;
      if (state.images.length >= TOPUP_TARGET) return;
      if (state.generating) return;
      // Defer slightly so we don't race the just-shown image's transition
      setTimeout(() => {
        if (!state.enabled) return;
        const need = TOPUP_TARGET - state.images.length;
        if (need > 0) generateMore(need);
      }, 800);
    }

    // ----------------------------------------------------------------
    // Display + Ken Burns transition
    // ----------------------------------------------------------------

    function showCurrent() {
      if (!state.container || !state.images.length) return;
      const cur = state.images[state.cursor % state.images.length];
      if (!cur) return;
      const newEl = state.slides[state.slot];
      const oldEl = state.slides[1 - state.slot];
      if (!newEl) return;
      runTransition(oldEl, newEl, cur.dataUrl);
    }

    /**
     * Pick a transition (Ken Burns weighted ~60%, others share the rest)
     * and dispatch to its run() handler. Falls back to Ken Burns if the
     * picker yields a missing entry.
     */
    function runTransition(oldEl, newEl, url) {
      const id = TRANSITION_PICKER[Math.floor(Math.random() * TRANSITION_PICKER.length)];
      const tx = TRANSITIONS[id] || TRANSITIONS.kenBurns;
      const fadeSec     = DEFAULT_FADE_SEC;
      const intervalSec = state.intervalSec;
      try {
        tx.run(oldEl, newEl, url, fadeSec, intervalSec);
      } catch (e) {
        // Defensive: if a custom transition throws, fall back to Ken Burns
        // so the slideshow keeps progressing instead of freezing.
        console.warn('[memHero bgSlideshow] transition failed, falling back:', id, e);
        TRANSITIONS.kenBurns.run(oldEl, newEl, url, fadeSec, intervalSec);
      }
    }

    function scheduleNext() {
      clearTimeout(state.advanceTimer);
      state.advanceTimer = setTimeout(() => {
        advance();
      }, state.intervalSec * 1000);
    }

    function advance() {
      if (!state.enabled || !state.container) return;
      if (state.images.length === 0) {
        // Still empty — try again later, generation may catch up
        scheduleNext();
        return;
      }
      state.cursor = (state.cursor + 1) % state.images.length;
      state.slot = 1 - state.slot;
      showCurrent();
      scheduleNext();
      queueTopUp();
    }

    // ----------------------------------------------------------------
    // Settings persistence
    // ----------------------------------------------------------------

    function saveCfg() {
      try {
        const s = loadSettings();
        s.chatBgSlideshow = {
          enabled:        state.enabled,
          prompt:         state.prompt,
          intervalSec:    state.intervalSec,
          opacity:        state.opacity,
          activeStyleIds: state.activeStyleIds.slice(),
        };
        saveSettings(s);
      } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
    }

    // ----------------------------------------------------------------
    // Prompt enhancer — streams an aiTextPlugin rewrite into the
    // textarea. Cancels itself on second click. Adapted from AI
    // Slideshow's runEnhance (perchance.org/ai-slideshow ~L2693).
    // ----------------------------------------------------------------

    /**
     * Run the prompt enhancer against the textarea. Streams chunks back
     * in real time. Click cancels by bumping a token; the in-flight call
     * sees the stale token and bails on its next chunk.
     *
     * @param {HTMLTextAreaElement} promptTextarea
     * @param {HTMLButtonElement}   enhanceBtn
     * @param {'short'|'medium'}    length
     */
    async function runEnhance(promptTextarea, enhanceBtn, length) {
      if (!promptTextarea || !enhanceBtn) return;
      // Toggle-cancel if already running.
      if (_enhanceRunning) {
        _enhanceToken++;
        _enhanceRunning = false;
        enhanceBtn.disabled = false;
        enhanceBtn.textContent = '✨';
        return;
      }
      const r = (typeof window !== 'undefined') ? window.root : null;
      if (!r || typeof r.aiTextPlugin !== 'function') {
        // No-op when aiTextPlugin isn't loaded — bg_slideshow keeps working,
        // user just can't enhance prompts.
        enhanceBtn.title = 'Prompt enhancer needs aiTextPlugin';
        return;
      }
      const original = (promptTextarea.value || '').trim();
      if (!original) {
        enhanceBtn.title = 'Type a casual idea first — the enhancer rewrites it';
        return;
      }
