      const instr = ENHANCE_INSTRUCTIONS[length] || ENHANCE_INSTRUCTIONS.medium;
      const myToken = ++_enhanceToken;
      _enhanceRunning = true;
      enhanceBtn.textContent = '⏹';
      enhanceBtn.title = 'Stop enhancing (revert)';

      promptTextarea.value = '';
      let acc = '';
      let streamObj = null;
      try {
        streamObj = r.aiTextPlugin({
          instruction: `${instr}\n\nUser Input: ${original}`,
          onChunk: (data) => {
            if (myToken !== _enhanceToken) {
              try { streamObj && streamObj.stop && streamObj.stop(); } catch {}
              throw new Error('cancelled');
            }
            if (data && data.isFromStartWith) return;
            acc += (data && data.textChunk) || '';
            promptTextarea.value = acc;
          },
        });
        const result = await streamObj;
        if (myToken !== _enhanceToken) return;
        // Some flows return the whole text on result.generatedText
        // without ever firing onChunk; pick whichever's non-empty.
        const finalText = (acc || (result && result.generatedText) || '').trim();
        if (!finalText) {
          promptTextarea.value = original;
          return;
        }
        // Strip stray surrounding quotes some models add despite instructions
        const cleaned = finalText.replace(/^["']|["']$/g, '');
        promptTextarea.value = cleaned;
        state.prompt = cleaned;
        saveCfg();
      } catch (e) {
        // On cancel or any failure, restore original — we never destroy
        // the user's text. Empty acc is the cancel case.
        promptTextarea.value = original;
        if (e && e.message !== 'cancelled') {
          console.warn('[memHero bgSlideshow] enhance failed:', e && e.message);
        }
      } finally {
        if (myToken === _enhanceToken) {
          _enhanceRunning = false;
          enhanceBtn.disabled = false;
          enhanceBtn.textContent = '✨';
          enhanceBtn.title = 'Enhance prompt with AI';
        }
      }
    }

    // ----------------------------------------------------------------
    // UI — 🎬 button + popover, mounted next to the static-bg button
    // ----------------------------------------------------------------

    function buildUI() {
      const container = document.createElement('div');
      container.className = 'pf-presets-container';
      container.style.cssText = 'position:relative;display:inline-block;';

      const btn = document.createElement('button');
      btn.type = 'button';
      btn.className = 'pf-export-btn';
      btn.textContent = '🎬';
      btn.title = 'Slideshow background (AI-generated)';

      const dropdown = document.createElement('div');
      dropdown.className = 'pf-presets-dropdown';
      dropdown.hidden = true;
      dropdown.style.cssText = 'min-width:280px;padding:10px;';

      // ---- Enable toggle ----
      const toggleRow = document.createElement('label');
      toggleRow.style.cssText = 'display:flex;align-items:center;gap:6px;cursor:pointer;'
                              + 'margin-bottom:6px;font-size:13px;';
      const toggleInput = document.createElement('input');
      toggleInput.type = 'checkbox';
      toggleInput.checked = state.enabled;
      toggleRow.appendChild(toggleInput);
      const toggleText = document.createElement('span');
      toggleText.textContent = ' Enable image slideshow';
      toggleRow.appendChild(toggleText);
      toggleInput.addEventListener('change', () => {
        state.enabled = toggleInput.checked;
        saveCfg();
        if (state.enabled) startSlideshow();
        else stopSlideshow();
      });
      dropdown.appendChild(toggleRow);

      // ---- Prompt textarea ----
      const promptLbl = document.createElement('div');
      promptLbl.style.cssText = 'font-size:11px;opacity:0.65;margin:8px 0 2px;';
      promptLbl.textContent = 'Image prompt';
      dropdown.appendChild(promptLbl);

      const promptInput = document.createElement('textarea');
      promptInput.value = state.prompt;
      promptInput.rows = 2;
      promptInput.placeholder = DEFAULT_PROMPT;
      promptInput.style.cssText = 'width:100%;resize:vertical;min-height:46px;'
                                + 'font-size:12px;font-family:inherit;'
                                + 'background:rgba(0,0,0,0.2);color:inherit;'
                                + 'border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12);'
                                + 'border-radius:4px;padding:6px;box-sizing:border-box;';
      promptInput.addEventListener('input', () => {
        const v = promptInput.value.trim();
        state.prompt = v || DEFAULT_PROMPT;
        saveCfg();
      });
      dropdown.appendChild(promptInput);

      // ---- Enhance row (✨ short / ✨+ medium) ----
      const enhanceRow = document.createElement('div');
      enhanceRow.style.cssText = 'display:flex;gap:6px;margin-top:6px;';

      const enhanceShortBtn = document.createElement('button');
      enhanceShortBtn.type = 'button';
      enhanceShortBtn.textContent = '✨';
      enhanceShortBtn.title = 'Enhance prompt (short, ~300-600 chars)';
      enhanceShortBtn.style.cssText = 'flex:1;padding:5px;cursor:pointer;font-size:13px;';
      enhanceShortBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
        runEnhance(promptInput, enhanceShortBtn, 'short');
      });
      enhanceRow.appendChild(enhanceShortBtn);

      const enhanceMedBtn = document.createElement('button');
      enhanceMedBtn.type = 'button';
      enhanceMedBtn.textContent = '✨+';
      enhanceMedBtn.title = 'Enhance prompt (medium, ~600-1100 chars)';
      enhanceMedBtn.style.cssText = 'flex:1;padding:5px;cursor:pointer;font-size:13px;';
      enhanceMedBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
        runEnhance(promptInput, enhanceMedBtn, 'medium');
      });
      enhanceRow.appendChild(enhanceMedBtn);

      const restoreBtn = document.createElement('button');
      restoreBtn.type = 'button';
      restoreBtn.textContent = '↺';
      restoreBtn.title = 'Restore default prompt';
      restoreBtn.style.cssText = 'padding:5px 10px;cursor:pointer;font-size:13px;opacity:0.75;';
      restoreBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
        promptInput.value = DEFAULT_PROMPT;
        state.prompt = DEFAULT_PROMPT;
        saveCfg();
      });
      enhanceRow.appendChild(restoreBtn);
      dropdown.appendChild(enhanceRow);

      // ---- Style preset chips ----
      const stylesLbl = document.createElement('div');
      stylesLbl.style.cssText = 'font-size:11px;opacity:0.65;margin:10px 0 4px;';
      stylesLbl.textContent = 'Style presets (click to toggle)';
      dropdown.appendChild(stylesLbl);

      const chipsRow = document.createElement('div');
      chipsRow.style.cssText = 'display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:4px;';
      const chipNodes = {};
      function refreshChipStyles() {
        for (const id of Object.keys(chipNodes)) {
          const isActive = state.activeStyleIds.includes(id);
          const node = chipNodes[id];
          node.style.background = isActive
            ? 'rgba(216, 179, 106, 0.22)'    // amber-tinted active
            : 'rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06)';
          node.style.borderColor = isActive
            ? 'rgba(216, 179, 106, 0.55)'
            : 'rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.14)';
          node.style.color = isActive
            ? 'var(--pf-accent-hi, #e8c97a)'
            : 'inherit';
        }
      }
      for (const p of STYLE_PRESETS) {
        const chip = document.createElement('button');
        chip.type = 'button';
        chip.textContent = p.label;
        chip.title = p.prompt;
        chip.style.cssText = 'border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.14);'
                           + 'border-radius:14px;padding:3px 9px;font-size:11px;'
                           + 'cursor:pointer;background:rgba(255,255,255,0.06);'
                           + 'color:inherit;transition:background 0.15s, border-color 0.15s, color 0.15s;';
        chip.addEventListener('click', () => {
          const idx = state.activeStyleIds.indexOf(p.id);
          if (idx >= 0) state.activeStyleIds.splice(idx, 1);
          else state.activeStyleIds.push(p.id);
          refreshChipStyles();
          saveCfg();
        });
        chipNodes[p.id] = chip;
        chipsRow.appendChild(chip);
      }
      refreshChipStyles();
      dropdown.appendChild(chipsRow);

      // ---- Interval slider ----
      const intervalLbl = document.createElement('div');
      intervalLbl.style.cssText = 'font-size:11px;opacity:0.65;margin:8px 0 2px;'
                                + 'display:flex;justify-content:space-between;';
      const intervalLblText = document.createElement('span');
      intervalLblText.textContent = 'Interval';
      const intervalLblVal = document.createElement('span');
      intervalLblVal.textContent = state.intervalSec + 's';
      intervalLbl.appendChild(intervalLblText);
      intervalLbl.appendChild(intervalLblVal);
      dropdown.appendChild(intervalLbl);

      const intervalInput = document.createElement('input');
      intervalInput.type = 'range';
      intervalInput.min = '6';
      intervalInput.max = '30';
      intervalInput.step = '1';
      intervalInput.value = String(state.intervalSec);
      intervalInput.style.width = '100%';
      intervalInput.addEventListener('input', () => {
        state.intervalSec = Number(intervalInput.value);
        intervalLblVal.textContent = state.intervalSec + 's';
        saveCfg();
        // Reschedule with the new interval so the change feels immediate
        if (state.enabled && state.container) {
          scheduleNext();
        }
      });
      dropdown.appendChild(intervalInput);

      // ---- Opacity slider ----
      const opacityLbl = document.createElement('div');
      opacityLbl.style.cssText = 'font-size:11px;opacity:0.65;margin:8px 0 2px;'
                               + 'display:flex;justify-content:space-between;';
      const opacityLblText = document.createElement('span');
      opacityLblText.textContent = 'Opacity';
      const opacityLblVal = document.createElement('span');
      opacityLblVal.textContent = Math.round(state.opacity * 100) + '%';
      opacityLbl.appendChild(opacityLblText);
      opacityLbl.appendChild(opacityLblVal);
      dropdown.appendChild(opacityLbl);

      const opacityInput = document.createElement('input');
      opacityInput.type = 'range';
      opacityInput.min = '0.10';
      opacityInput.max = '0.80';
      opacityInput.step = '0.05';
      opacityInput.value = String(state.opacity);
      opacityInput.style.width = '100%';
      opacityInput.addEventListener('input', () => {
        state.opacity = Number(opacityInput.value);
        opacityLblVal.textContent = Math.round(state.opacity * 100) + '%';
        saveCfg();
        // Live update: nudge the visible slide's opacity so the user sees
        // the change without waiting for the next advance.
        const visible = state.slides[state.slot];
        if (visible && visible.style.opacity !== '0') {
          visible.style.opacity = String(state.opacity);
        }
      });
      dropdown.appendChild(opacityInput);

      // ---- Action buttons ----
      const actionsRow = document.createElement('div');
      actionsRow.style.cssText = 'display:flex;gap:6px;margin-top:10px;';

      const genBtn = document.createElement('button');
      genBtn.type = 'button';
      genBtn.textContent = '+ Generate';
      genBtn.style.cssText = 'flex:1;padding:6px;cursor:pointer;font-size:12px;';
      genBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
        const orig = genBtn.textContent;
        genBtn.disabled = true;
        genBtn.textContent = '⏳ ...';
        try {
          const dataUrl = await generateOneImage();
          if (dataUrl) {
            state.images.push({ dataUrl, prompt: state.prompt });
            // If newly cached image is the FIRST and we're enabled, show it
            if (state.enabled && state.images.length === 1) {
              ensureContainer();
              state.cursor = 0;
              state.slot = 0;
              showCurrent();
              scheduleNext();
            }
            updateClearLabel();
            genBtn.textContent = '✅';
          } else {
            genBtn.textContent = '⚠';
          }
        } finally {
          setTimeout(() => {
            genBtn.disabled = false;
            genBtn.textContent = orig;
          }, 1200);
        }
      });
      actionsRow.appendChild(genBtn);

      const clearBtn = document.createElement('button');
      clearBtn.type = 'button';
      clearBtn.style.cssText = 'flex:1;padding:6px;cursor:pointer;font-size:12px;opacity:0.75;';
      function updateClearLabel() {
        clearBtn.textContent = '🗑 Clear (' + state.images.length + ')';
      }
      updateClearLabel();
      clearBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
        state.images = [];
        state.cursor = 0;
        updateClearLabel();
        // If running, immediately blank the slides and re-generate
        if (state.enabled && state.container) {
          for (const slide of state.slides) {
            if (slide) {
              slide.style.transition = 'opacity 0.4s ease';
              slide.style.opacity = '0';
              slide.style.backgroundImage = '';
            }
          }
          generateMore(2);
        }
      });
      actionsRow.appendChild(clearBtn);

      dropdown.appendChild(actionsRow);

      // ---- Hint footer ----
      const hint = document.createElement('div');
      hint.style.cssText = 'font-size:10.5px;opacity:0.55;margin-top:8px;line-height:1.4;';
      hint.textContent = 'Soft Ken Burns + 4 alternate transitions (crossfade, slow zoom, '
                       + 'blur fade, parallax pan). Images kept in memory only (cap '
                       + MAX_IMAGES + '), regenerated on reload.';
      dropdown.appendChild(hint);

      // Assemble + mount
      container.appendChild(btn);
      container.appendChild(dropdown);
      const header = document.querySelector('.chat-header-right') ||
                     document.querySelector('.chat-header');
      if (header) header.appendChild(container);

      btn.addEventListener('click', () => { dropdown.hidden = !dropdown.hidden; });
      document.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
        if (!container.contains(e.target)) dropdown.hidden = true;
      });

      // Light polling refresh of the cleared-count label so background
      // top-ups appear in the popover without needing a re-open.
      setInterval(() => {
        if (!dropdown.hidden) updateClearLabel();
      }, 2000);
    }

    // ----------------------------------------------------------------
    // CSS — body-level fixed container, two slides, soft tint overlay
    // ----------------------------------------------------------------

    function injectCSS() {
      if (document.getElementById('pf-bgss-styles')) return;
      const style = document.createElement('style');
      style.id = 'pf-bgss-styles';
      // The overlay tint matches the navy/gold trim palette so the
      // slideshow blends with the new theme. RGBA values are derived
      // from --pf-theme-primary (#0d1b2e). The tint also gives messages
      // a consistent dark backdrop regardless of which generated image
      // is currently visible — text stays readable.
      //
      // Layering strategy (intentionally minimal):
      //   - slideshow uses z-index: -1, so it's behind ALL content
      //     without us having to touch any other element. Modals,
      //     popups, sidebars all paint over it normally.
      //   - html background is set to --pf-theme-primary so the page
      //     has the themed navy base when the slideshow is OFF (or
      //     between generations). The slideshow div paints over the
      //     html bg at level z=-1 when ON.
      //   - body background is forced transparent so the slideshow
      //     (which is body-level fixed at z=-1) is actually visible
      //     through body's painting layer.
      //
      // Earlier rounds tried `body > *:not(.pf-bgss-container) { z-index: 1 }`
      // to lift content above an at-z-index-0 slideshow. That rule
      // created new stacking contexts on every body child, which TRAPPED
      // modal popups (Profile, Memory & Lore, etc.) inside their
      // ancestor's stacking context — they couldn't expand to viewport
      // and their themed panel rendered at an unintended z-layer. The
      // negative-z approach below avoids all of that.
      style.textContent =
        'html { background-color: var(--pf-theme-primary, #0d1b2e); }'
        + 'body { background-color: transparent !important; }'
        + '.pf-bgss-container { '
          + 'position: fixed !important; inset: 0; z-index: -1; '
          + 'pointer-events: none; overflow: hidden; '
        + '}'
        + '.pf-bgss-slide { '
          + 'position: absolute; inset: 0; '
          + 'background-size: cover; background-position: center; '
          + 'background-repeat: no-repeat; '
          + 'opacity: 0; will-change: opacity, transform; '
          + 'transform-origin: center center; '
          + 'backface-visibility: hidden; '
        + '}'
        + '.pf-bgss-overlay { '
          + 'position: absolute; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; '
          + 'background: linear-gradient(to bottom, '
            + 'rgba(13, 27, 46, 0.18) 0%, '
            + 'rgba(13, 27, 46, 0.38) 100%); '
        + '}';
      document.head.appendChild(style);
    }
  }

  /* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     module: chat/character_portraits.js
     ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  // chat/character_portraits.js
  //
  // Auto-generate avatar portraits for characters that don't have one.
  // Adapted from HNE's "persona portrait grid hydration" pattern (HNE
  // generates portraits for each persona card on demand using
  // textToImagePlugin against the persona's tone/voice description).
  //
  // For Memory Hero, the input is the character's `roleInstruction`
  // distilled into a short visual prompt. The result data URL is saved
  // back into the character record (window.db.characters.update).
  //
  // The button is mounted into the chat header next to the existing
  // 🎬 / 🏞 backdrop tools — it acts on the active thread's character.
  // For batch portrait generation across the whole library, this could
  // be extended to iterate db.characters.toArray() and skip ones that
  // already have an avatarUrl; left as a future follow-up.
  //
  // Bootstrap: idempotent.

  function initCharacterPortraits() {
    if (initCharacterPortraits._done) return;
    initCharacterPortraits._done = true;

    const NEGATIVE = 'text, watermark, logo, deformed face, distorted anatomy, '
                   + 'extra limbs, low quality, blurry, signature, ui elements, '
                   + 'multiple subjects, frame, border';

    /**
     * Build a portrait prompt from a character's role instruction. We
     * keep it short and visual — the role instruction is often long and
     * full of behavioural rules ("never break character", "respond in
     * third person") that aren't useful for an image model. We trim to
     * the first 280 chars and prepend a portrait framing template.
     */
    function buildPortraitPrompt(character) {
      const role = String((character && character.roleInstruction) || '')
                    .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
                    .trim()
                    .slice(0, 280);
      const name = String((character && character.name) || 'character').trim();
      // The framing terms are deliberately neutral — we want the
      // character's own description to do the work, not impose a
      // particular art style. Style overrides can be applied via the
      // bg_slideshow chip system if the user wants a uniform look.
      return `portrait of ${name}, character study, head and shoulders, `
           + `soft natural lighting, neutral background, painterly. `
           + `Character description: ${role}`;
    }

    /**
     * Look up the active thread's character via upstream's Dexie
     * instance. Returns { thread, character } or null on any failure.
     * Defensive at every step so a missing object doesn't crash the UI.
     */
    async function getActiveCharacter() {
      try {
        const db = window.db;
        if (!db || !db.threads || !db.characters) return null;
        // Upstream stores active thread id either on window.activeThreadId
        // or via a CSS class on a sidebar element. Fall back across both.
        let threadId = window.activeThreadId;
        if (threadId == null) {
          const selected = document.querySelector('.thread.selected, .thread-item.selected, [data-thread-id].selected');
          if (selected) {
            const tid = selected.getAttribute('data-thread-id');
            threadId = tid != null ? Number(tid) : null;
          }
        }
        if (threadId == null) return null;
        const thread = await db.threads.get(threadId);
        if (!thread) return null;
        const charId = (thread.character && thread.character.id != null)
                        ? thread.character.id
                        : thread.characterId;
        if (charId == null) return null;
        const character = await db.characters.get(charId);
        if (!character) return null;
        return { thread, character };
      } catch (e) {
        console.warn('[memHero portraits] getActiveCharacter failed:', e && e.message);
        return null;
      }
    }

    /**
     * Generate ONE portrait for the given character via textToImagePlugin
     * and write it back into the characters store. Returns the dataUrl
     * on success, null on failure. Strict {prompt, negative} contract.
     */
    async function generatePortraitFor(character) {
      const r = window.root;
      if (!r || typeof r.textToImagePlugin !== 'function') return null;
      try {
        const result = await r.textToImagePlugin({
          prompt:   buildPortraitPrompt(character),
          negative: NEGATIVE,
        });
        const dataUrl = (result && result.dataUrl) || (typeof result === 'string' ? result : null);
        if (typeof dataUrl !== 'string' || !dataUrl.startsWith('data:')) return null;
        // Persist to the characters store. db.characters.update returns
        // a promise that resolves when the write is durable.
        try {
          await window.db.characters.update(character.id, { avatarUrl: dataUrl });
        } catch (e) {
          console.warn('[memHero portraits] update failed (returning dataUrl anyway):', e && e.message);
        }
        return dataUrl;
      } catch (e) {
        console.warn('[memHero portraits] generation failed:', e && e.message);
        return null;
      }
    }

    /**
     * Mount the 🖼️ button. It runs against whichever character the
     * active thread points to. Visual feedback: ⏳ while generating,
     * ✅ on success, ⚠ on failure, then reverts after a moment.
     */
    function mountButton() {
      if (document.querySelector('.pf-charport-btn')) return; // idempotent
      const btn = document.createElement('button');
      btn.type = 'button';
      btn.className = 'pf-export-btn pf-charport-btn';
      btn.textContent = '🖼️';
      btn.title = 'Generate AI portrait for the current character';
      btn.style.marginLeft = '4px';

      btn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
        if (btn.disabled) return;
        btn.disabled = true;
        const orig = btn.textContent;
        btn.textContent = '⏳';
        try {
          const ctx = await getActiveCharacter();
          if (!ctx) {
            btn.textContent = '⚠';
            btn.title = 'No active character — open a thread first';
          } else {
            // If the character already has a non-empty avatar, confirm
            // overwrite via the title (we don't want a confirm dialog
            // every click; users can see existing avatar in the chat).
            const dataUrl = await generatePortraitFor(ctx.character);
            if (dataUrl) {
              btn.textContent = '✅';
              btn.title = 'Portrait generated and saved';
            } else {
              btn.textContent = '⚠';
              btn.title = 'Portrait generation failed';
            }
          }
        } finally {
          setTimeout(() => {
            btn.disabled = false;
            btn.textContent = orig;
            btn.title = 'Generate AI portrait for the current character';
          }, 1800);
        }
      });

      const header = document.querySelector('.chat-header-right') ||
                     document.querySelector('.chat-header');
      if (header) header.appendChild(btn);
    }

    // Mount once chat header is present. Poll briefly because the chat
    // header may render asynchronously after upstream loads threads.
    function tryMount(remaining) {
      mountButton();
      if (!document.querySelector('.pf-charport-btn') && remaining > 0) {
        setTimeout(() => tryMount(remaining - 1), 400);
      }
    }
    tryMount(20); // up to ~8 seconds of retry, then give up silently

    // Expose for programmatic use (e.g. batch hydration scripts later)
    if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
      window.__memHero_generatePortraitFor = generatePortraitFor;
      window.__memHero_buildPortraitPrompt = buildPortraitPrompt;
    }
  }

  /* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     module: chat/reimagine.js
     ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  // chat/reimagine.js
  //
  // 🎭 "Reimagine" button on every message — rewrites the message text
  // in the active character's voice (or the user's persona voice for
  // user messages). Adapted from HNE's "✨ Reimagine in your guide's
  // voice" pattern (HNE applies this to custom session blocks; we apply
  // it to chat messages).
  //
  // Behavior:
  //   1. Click 🎭 next to a message
  //   2. A small floating popover shows: "Reimagine in <character>'s voice"
  //      with a confirm + cancel
  //   3. On confirm: stream a rewrite via aiTextPlugin into the message
  //      text, replacing it. Empty-on-failure restores original.
  //
  // Storage: rewritten message goes back through upstream's
  // db.messages.update so it sticks. The original is preserved in the
  // message's `editHistory` if upstream tracks it; otherwise it's lost
  // (chat is fundamentally lossy on rewrites).
  //
  // Bootstrap: idempotent. Uses MutationObserver to add buttons to new
  // messages as they're rendered.

  function initReimagine() {
    if (initReimagine._done) return;
    initReimagine._done = true;

    const SHORT_INSTR = 'ROLE: Skilled prose stylist.\n'
      + 'ACTION: Rewrite the user message below in the voice of the character described, '
      + 'preserving meaning and intent but matching their tone, vocabulary, '
      + 'cadence, and emotional register. Same approximate length.\n'
      + 'FORBIDDEN: explanatory preamble, quotes around the result, narration about '
      + 'what you changed, instructional language.\n'
      + 'OUTPUT: ONLY the rewritten message text. No preamble. No quotes.';

    /**
     * Find the chat character — first try upstream's active thread, fall
     * back to the most-recently-seen non-user message author in the DOM.
     */
    async function getCharacterForActiveThread() {
      try {
        const db = window.db;
        if (db && db.threads && db.characters) {
          const tid = window.activeThreadId;
          if (tid != null) {
            const thread = await db.threads.get(tid);
            const charId = thread && (thread.character?.id ?? thread.characterId);
            if (charId != null) {
              const c = await db.characters.get(charId);
              if (c) return c;
            }
          }
        }
      } catch {}
      return null;
    }

    /**
     * Rewrite a piece of text in the character's voice. Returns the
     * rewritten string, or null on failure / cancellation.
     */
    async function rewriteText(text, character) {
      const r = window.root;
      if (!r || typeof r.aiTextPlugin !== 'function') return null;
      const role = String((character && character.roleInstruction) || '')
                    .replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim().slice(0, 600);
      const name = String((character && character.name) || 'the character').trim();
      const instruction =
        `${SHORT_INSTR}\n\n` +
        `Character name: ${name}\n` +
        `Character description: ${role}\n\n` +
        `Original message:\n${text}`;
      try {
        const result = await r.aiTextPlugin({
          instruction,
          stopSequences: ['\n\n[[', '\n[['],
        });
        const out = (result && result.generatedText || '').trim().replace(/^["']|["']$/g, '');
        return out || null;
      } catch (e) {
        console.warn('[memHero reimagine] aiTextPlugin failed:', e && e.message);
        return null;
      }
    }

    /**
     * Add a 🎭 button to a message element if it doesn't already have one.
     * Called from the MutationObserver as new messages render.
     */
    function decorateMessageEl(msgEl) {
      if (!msgEl || msgEl._pfReimagineDone) return;
      msgEl._pfReimagineDone = true;

      const btn = document.createElement('button');
      btn.type = 'button';
      btn.className = 'pf-reimagine-btn emojiButton';
      btn.textContent = '🎭';
      btn.title = 'Reimagine this message in the character\'s voice';
      // .emojiButton (upstream ~line 1530) provides opacity:0.5 +
      // cursor:pointer + :hover{opacity:1}. We only override <button> UA
      // chrome and add the inline sizing the existing row icons use.
      btn.style.cssText = 'background:none;border:none;'
                       + 'padding:0;line-height:1;color:inherit;'
                       + 'font-size:0.8rem;margin-left:1rem;'
                       + 'display:flex;align-items:center;';
      btn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
        e.stopPropagation();
        handleReimagineClick(msgEl, btn);
      });

      // Position: same neighborhood as the narrate button — inside the
      // unnamed flex wrapper that holds .recomputeButton, immediately
      // after it. Final visual order ends up:
      //
      //   ✏️ 🗑️ 🔁 🔊 🎭 🙄 [variants]
      //
      // (narrate inits later than reimagine, so by the time narrate
      // inserts, reimagine is already there. Both go right after 🔁,
      // so narrate ends up directly adjacent to 🔁 and reimagine is
      // pushed one slot further right.)
      const recomputeBtn = msgEl.querySelector('.recomputeButton');
      if (recomputeBtn && recomputeBtn.parentNode) {
        recomputeBtn.parentNode.insertBefore(btn, recomputeBtn.nextSibling);
      } else {
        const fallback = msgEl.querySelector('.info') || msgEl;
        fallback.appendChild(btn);
      }
    }

    /**
     * Find the message body text node within a rendered message. Tries
     * known upstream selectors; falls back to the message element itself.
     */
    function findMessageBody(msgEl) {
      return msgEl.querySelector('.messageText, .message-text, .msg-body, .msg-content')
          || msgEl;
    }

    /** Find the message id from the rendered element. */
    function findMessageId(msgEl) {
      const fromAttr = msgEl.getAttribute('data-message-id') ||
                       msgEl.getAttribute('data-msg-id') ||
                       msgEl.getAttribute('data-id');
      const n = fromAttr != null ? Number(fromAttr) : NaN;
      return Number.isFinite(n) ? n : null;
    }

    async function handleReimagineClick(msgEl, btn) {
      if (btn.disabled) return;
      btn.disabled = true;
      const origLabel = btn.textContent;
      btn.textContent = '⏳';
      try {
        const body = findMessageBody(msgEl);
        const original = (body.textContent || '').trim();
        if (!original) { btn.textContent = '⚠'; return; }

        const character = await getCharacterForActiveThread();
        if (!character) {
          btn.textContent = '⚠';
          btn.title = 'No active character — open a thread first';
          return;
        }

        const rewritten = await rewriteText(original, character);
        if (!rewritten) { btn.textContent = '⚠'; return; }

        // Apply visually first for instant feedback
        body.textContent = rewritten;

        // Persist to DB if we can identify the message
        const msgId = findMessageId(msgEl);
        if (msgId != null && window.db && window.db.messages) {
          try {
            await window.db.messages.update(msgId, { content: rewritten });
          } catch (e) {
            console.warn('[memHero reimagine] db.update failed:', e && e.message);
          }
        }
        btn.textContent = '✅';
      } finally {
        setTimeout(() => {
          btn.disabled = false;
          btn.textContent = origLabel;
        }, 1500);
      }
    }

    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    // MutationObserver — decorate every message as it appears.
    // ------------------------------------------------------------------

    function scanAndDecorate(root) {
      const msgs = root.querySelectorAll('.message, .chat-message, [data-message-id]');
      msgs.forEach(decorateMessageEl);
    }

    function watch() {
      const chatEl = document.getElementById('chatMessagesEl') ||
                     document.querySelector('.chat-messages, .chat-area');
      if (!chatEl) {
        // Try again shortly — chat may not be mounted yet
        setTimeout(watch, 800);
        return;
      }
      scanAndDecorate(chatEl);
      const obs = new MutationObserver((muts) => {
        for (const m of muts) {
          for (const node of m.addedNodes || []) {
            if (node.nodeType !== 1) continue;
            // The added node may BE a message or contain new ones
            if (node.matches && node.matches('.message, .chat-message, [data-message-id]')) {
              decorateMessageEl(node);
            } else {
              scanAndDecorate(node);
            }
          }
        }
      });
      obs.observe(chatEl, { childList: true, subtree: true });
    }

    watch();

    // Expose for programmatic use
    if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
      window.__memHero_reimagineText = rewriteText;
    }
  }

  /* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     module: chat/bug_report.js
     ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  // chat/bug_report.js
  //
  // Surfaces the already-imported bug-report-plugin (perchance.org's
  // bug-report-plugin) via a small 🐞 button. Adapted from HNE's
  // bug-report integration (HNE uses it for opt-in user-initiated bug
  // reports — never silent telemetry).
  //
  // The plugin gathers browser version, localStorage size limits,
  // viewport, user agent, etc. and produces a markdown blob suitable
  // for pasting into a forum / GitHub issue / comments thread. We just
  // give it a button and copy the result to clipboard.
  //
  // The plugin is imported as `bugReport` in memory_hero_1.txt. If it's
  // not available at runtime (e.g. Perchance plugin hosting is down),
  // the button still works — we fall back to a minimal navigator dump.
  //
  // Bootstrap: idempotent.

  function initBugReport() {
    if (initBugReport._done) return;
    initBugReport._done = true;

    function fallbackInfo() {
      // Minimal report when bugReport plugin isn't reachable. Includes
      // the bare facts a forum helper would ask for first.
      const lines = [];
      lines.push('**Memory Hero bug report**');
      lines.push('');
      lines.push('User-Agent: `' + (navigator && navigator.userAgent || 'unknown') + '`');
      lines.push('Language: `' + (navigator && navigator.language || 'unknown') + '`');
      lines.push('Viewport: `' + window.innerWidth + 'x' + window.innerHeight + '`');
      lines.push('Pixel ratio: `' + (window.devicePixelRatio || 1) + '`');
      lines.push('Online: `' + (navigator && navigator.onLine) + '`');
      lines.push('Page load time: `' + (window.pageLoadStartTime ? (Date.now() - window.pageLoadStartTime) + 'ms' : 'n/a') + '`');
      lines.push('Cookies enabled: `' + (navigator && navigator.cookieEnabled) + '`');
      lines.push('Storage estimate: pending...');
      lines.push('');
      lines.push('Steps to reproduce: ');
      lines.push('1. ');
      lines.push('2. ');
      lines.push('');
      lines.push('Expected vs. actual: ');
      lines.push('');
      return lines.join('\n');
    }

    async function gatherReport() {
      // First-class path: use root.bugReport if the plugin loaded.
      const r = window.root;
      try {
        if (r && r.bugReport && typeof r.bugReport === 'function') {
          const out = await r.bugReport();
          if (typeof out === 'string' && out.length) return out;
        }
        // Some versions expose .getInfo() or .text() — try those too
        if (r && r.bugReport && typeof r.bugReport.getInfo === 'function') {
          const out = await r.bugReport.getInfo();
          if (typeof out === 'string' && out.length) return out;
        }
      } catch (e) {
        console.warn('[memHero bugReport] plugin call failed, using fallback:', e && e.message);
      }
      // Fallback: build something minimally useful ourselves
      let extra = '';
      try {
        if (navigator.storage && navigator.storage.estimate) {
          const est = await navigator.storage.estimate();
          extra = '\n\nStorage usage: `' + (est.usage || 0) + ' / ' + (est.quota || 0) + ' bytes`';
        }
      } catch {}
      return fallbackInfo() + extra;
    }

    function mountButton() {
      if (document.querySelector('.pf-bugreport-btn')) return; // idempotent
      const btn = document.createElement('button');
      btn.type = 'button';
      btn.className = 'pf-export-btn pf-bugreport-btn';
      btn.textContent = '🐞';
      btn.title = 'Copy bug-report info to clipboard';
      btn.style.marginLeft = '4px';

      btn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
        if (btn.disabled) return;
        btn.disabled = true;
        const orig = btn.textContent;
        btn.textContent = '⏳';
        try {
          const report = await gatherReport();
          if (navigator.clipboard && navigator.clipboard.writeText) {
            await navigator.clipboard.writeText(report);
            btn.textContent = '📋';
            btn.title = 'Bug report copied to clipboard — paste it into the forum / GitHub issue';
          } else {
            // Clipboard API unavailable — show in a textarea modal instead
            showReportModal(report);
            btn.textContent = '📋';
          }
        } catch (e) {
          console.warn('[memHero bugReport] copy failed:', e && e.message);
          btn.textContent = '⚠';
        } finally {
          setTimeout(() => {
            btn.disabled = false;
            btn.textContent = orig;
            btn.title = 'Copy bug-report info to clipboard';
          }, 2000);
        }
      });

      const header = document.querySelector('.chat-header-right') ||
                     document.querySelector('.chat-header');
      if (header) header.appendChild(btn);
    }

    /**
     * Fallback modal for browsers/contexts where navigator.clipboard
     * isn't available (older Safari, file:// origins, etc.). Shows the
     * report text in a selectable textarea + a close button.
     */
    function showReportModal(reportText) {
      const overlay = document.createElement('div');
      overlay.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;inset:0;z-index:99999;'
                            + 'background:rgba(0,0,0,0.6);display:flex;'
                            + 'align-items:center;justify-content:center;padding:20px;';
      const card = document.createElement('div');
      card.style.cssText = 'background:#142847;color:#e8dcc4;border:1px solid #d8b36a;'
                         + 'border-radius:8px;padding:14px;max-width:560px;width:100%;'
                         + 'box-shadow:0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.6);';
      const title = document.createElement('div');
      title.textContent = 'Bug report (select all + copy)';
      title.style.cssText = 'font-weight:600;margin-bottom:8px;color:#d8b36a;';
      const ta = document.createElement('textarea');
      ta.value = reportText;
      ta.rows = 14;
      ta.style.cssText = 'width:100%;font-family:monospace;font-size:11px;'
                       + 'background:#0d1b2e;color:#e8dcc4;'
                       + 'border:1px solid rgba(216,179,106,0.4);'
                       + 'border-radius:4px;padding:8px;resize:vertical;box-sizing:border-box;';
      const closeBtn = document.createElement('button');
      closeBtn.type = 'button';
      closeBtn.textContent = 'Close';
      closeBtn.style.cssText = 'margin-top:10px;padding:6px 14px;cursor:pointer;'
                             + 'background:#d8b36a;color:#0d1b2e;border:none;'
                             + 'border-radius:4px;font-weight:600;';
      closeBtn.addEventListener('click', () => overlay.remove());
      overlay.addEventListener('click', (e) => { if (e.target === overlay) overlay.remove(); });
      card.appendChild(title);
      card.appendChild(ta);
      card.appendChild(closeBtn);
      overlay.appendChild(card);
      document.body.appendChild(overlay);
      try { ta.focus(); ta.select(); } catch {}
    }

    function tryMount(remaining) {
      mountButton();
      if (!document.querySelector('.pf-bugreport-btn') && remaining > 0) {
        setTimeout(() => tryMount(remaining - 1), 400);
      }
    }
    tryMount(20);
  }

  /* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     module: chat/narrate.js
     ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  // chat/narrate.js
  //
  // On-demand quote-aware text-to-speech for individual chat messages,
  // with optional dual-voice narration (one voice for the narrator's
  // prose, a different voice for "quoted speech").
  //
  // Distinct from chat/voice.js — that module's speaker toggle is a
  // PASSIVE auto-read of every new AI message with one default voice.
  // This module is ACTIVE per-message narration triggered by clicking
  // a 🔊 button in the message-action row, with full voice / rate /
  // pitch control and the dual-narrator split. The two modules are
  // complementary; they share the same Web Speech API but never the
  // same utterance.
  //
  // ─── Surfaces ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  //
  //   1. Per-message 🔊 button — mounted via MutationObserver into
  //      `.messageControls` next to the existing edit / delete /
  //      regenerate icons. Click to narrate; click again to stop.
  //
  //   2. "🔊 narrator settings…" entry injected into the existing
  //      `#threadOptionsPopup` (the ⚒️ options menu near the input).
  //      Opens the settings modal.
  //
  //   3. Settings modal — narrator-voice picker, optional character-
  //      voice picker (gated on dual-mode toggle), rate/pitch sliders
  //      for each voice, and a Test button.
  //
  // ─── Quote-aware splitter ──────────────────────────────────────────
  //
  // splitForNarration() walks the text once and yields {voice, text}
  // segments. We match three quote-pair styles in priority order:
  //   - ASCII   "..."      (most common in chat / fiction)
  //   - smart   \u201C\u201D  (Word / iOS auto-quotes)
  //   - guillemets «...»   (some EU localizations)
  // We do NOT split on single quotes — too many false positives from
  // possessives ("the king's army"). If the user wants this, it can be
  // added later; the regex is the only thing that needs touching.
  //
  // Unmatched / unbalanced quotes are NOT a bug case to bail on — we
  // just leave them as narrator text. Parsing must always succeed,
  // because the alternative is "your narration silently doesn't work".
  //
  // ─── Web Speech API quirks we handle ───────────────────────────────
  //
  //   - speechSynthesis.getVoices() returns [] before the voice list
  //     loads on Chrome / Firefox. We listen for the `voiceschanged`
  //     event and re-render the picker when it fires.
  //
  //   - speechSynthesis has its own utterance queue — calling speak()
  //     several times serializes them automatically. We exploit that
  //     to get clean dual-narration without manual scheduling.
  //
  //   - cancel() clears the queue immediately; we use this for stop.
  //
  // ─── Storage ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  //
  // settings.narrator = {
  //   enabled,                  // master toggle (button visible)
  //   dualMode,                 // split on quotes for character voice
  //   narratorVoiceURI,         // SpeechSynthesisVoice.voiceURI
  //   narratorRate,             // 0.5 – 1.5
  //   narratorPitch,            // 0.5 – 1.5
  //   characterVoiceURI,        // ditto, for quoted speech
  //   characterRate,
  //   characterPitch,
  // }
  //
  // Bootstrap: idempotent.

  function initNarrate() {
    if (initNarrate._done) return;
    initNarrate._done = true;

    if (!('speechSynthesis' in window)) return; // no TTS support

    // ---- Defaults ----
    const DEFAULTS = {
      enabled:           true,
      dualMode:          false,
      narratorVoiceURI:  '',
      narratorRate:      1.0,
      narratorPitch:     1.0,
      characterVoiceURI: '',
      characterRate:     1.0,
      characterPitch:    1.05,  // very slightly higher to differentiate by default
    };

    // ---- Load persisted settings ----
    function loadCfg() {
      try {
        const s = loadSettings();
        const cfg = (s && s.narrator) || {};
        return {
          enabled:           cfg.enabled !== false,
          dualMode:          !!cfg.dualMode,
          narratorVoiceURI:  String(cfg.narratorVoiceURI || ''),
          narratorRate:      clamp(cfg.narratorRate, 0.5, 1.5, DEFAULTS.narratorRate),
          narratorPitch:     clamp(cfg.narratorPitch, 0.5, 1.5, DEFAULTS.narratorPitch),
          characterVoiceURI: String(cfg.characterVoiceURI || ''),
          characterRate:     clamp(cfg.characterRate, 0.5, 1.5, DEFAULTS.characterRate),
          characterPitch:    clamp(cfg.characterPitch, 0.5, 1.5, DEFAULTS.characterPitch),
        };
      } catch { return { ...DEFAULTS }; }
    }
    function saveCfg(cfg) {
      try {
        const s = loadSettings();
        s.narrator = { ...cfg };
        saveSettings(s);
      } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
    }
    function clamp(n, lo, hi, fallback) {
      const v = Number(n);
      if (!Number.isFinite(v)) return fallback;
      return Math.max(lo, Math.min(hi, v));
    }

    let cfg = loadCfg();
    let currentlyNarratingBtn = null; // remembers which button to revert when narration ends/stops

    // ---- Voice list (handles async voiceschanged) ----

    let voicesCache = [];
    function refreshVoices() {
      try {
        voicesCache = window.speechSynthesis.getVoices() || [];
      } catch { voicesCache = []; }
    }
    refreshVoices();
    // Fires when the browser finishes loading the voice list (Chrome/Firefox)
    try {
      window.speechSynthesis.addEventListener('voiceschanged', refreshVoices);
    } catch { /* older browsers — no event support, voicesCache is whatever first call returned */ }

    function findVoice(uri) {
      if (!uri) return null;
      return voicesCache.find(v => v.voiceURI === uri) || null;
    }

    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Quote-aware splitter
    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    /**
     * Split text into [{voice:'narrator'|'character', text:string}, ...]
     * segments based on quote pairs. Skips empty segments. Always
     * returns at least one segment for non-empty input.
     *
     * Uses a single regex with a global flag and walks via lastIndex
     * for O(n) splitting — no backtracking, no exponential blowup on
     * pathological inputs.
     */
    function splitForNarration(rawText, useDualMode) {
      const text = String(rawText || '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
      if (!text) return [];
      if (!useDualMode) return [{ voice: 'narrator', text }];

      // Three quote-pair styles. Order matters only for performance,
      // not correctness — alternation tries each at the same position.
      // Lazy quantifier (.+?) so we always match the SHORTEST run
      // between paired quotes, not the longest.
      const QUOTE_RE = /"([^"]+?)"|\u201C([^\u201D]+?)\u201D|\u00AB([^\u00BB]+?)\u00BB/g;
      const segments = [];
      let lastIndex = 0;
      let m;
      while ((m = QUOTE_RE.exec(text)) !== null) {
        // Narrator prose preceding this quote
        if (m.index > lastIndex) {
          const pre = text.slice(lastIndex, m.index).trim();
          if (pre) segments.push({ voice: 'narrator', text: pre });
        }
        // The quoted speech itself (one of the three capture groups will be defined)
        const quoted = (m[1] || m[2] || m[3] || '').trim();
        if (quoted) segments.push({ voice: 'character', text: quoted });
        lastIndex = m.index + m[0].length;
      }
      // Trailing narrator prose after the last quote
      if (lastIndex < text.length) {
        const tail = text.slice(lastIndex).trim();
        if (tail) segments.push({ voice: 'narrator', text: tail });
      }
      // Defensive: if regex matched nothing (no quotes at all), the
      // whole string is narrator prose.
      if (segments.length === 0) segments.push({ voice: 'narrator', text });
      return segments;
    }

    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Strip markdown / code blocks / HTML before TTS
    // (mirrors chat/voice.js — same content, kept inline so we don't
    // rely on the speaker-toggle module being loaded.)
    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    function cleanForSpeech(raw) {
      let text = String(raw || '');
      text = text.replace(/```[\s\S]*?```/g, ''); // fenced code
      text = text.replace(/`[^`]+`/g, '');         // inline code
      text = text.replace(/\*\*([^*]+)\*\*/g, '$1'); // bold
      text = text.replace(/\*([^*]+)\*/g, '$1');     // italics
      text = text.replace(/[#_>`]/g, '');             // residual markdown punctuation
      text = text.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
      // Cap at a generous-but-bounded length so a runaway message can't
      // hold the speech queue hostage.
      if (text.length > 4000) text = text.slice(0, 4000) + '…';
      return text;
    }

    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Speak — dispatches segments to speechSynthesis with the right voice
    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    function speakSegments(segments, onAllDone) {
      // Cancel anything already queued so we don't pile narrations.
      try { window.speechSynthesis.cancel(); } catch {}
      const narratorVoice  = findVoice(cfg.narratorVoiceURI);
      const characterVoice = findVoice(cfg.characterVoiceURI) || narratorVoice;

      if (!segments.length) { if (onAllDone) onAllDone(); return; }

      let lastUtterance = null;
      for (const seg of segments) {
        const u = new SpeechSynthesisUtterance(seg.text);
        if (seg.voice === 'character') {
          if (characterVoice) u.voice = characterVoice;
          u.rate  = cfg.characterRate;
          u.pitch = cfg.characterPitch;
        } else {
          if (narratorVoice) u.voice = narratorVoice;
          u.rate  = cfg.narratorRate;
          u.pitch = cfg.narratorPitch;
        }
        try { window.speechSynthesis.speak(u); } catch (e) {
          console.warn('[memHero narrate] speak failed:', e && e.message);
        }
        lastUtterance = u;
      }
      // Fire "done" when the LAST utterance ends. This catches both
      // natural completion AND cancellation (cancel() triggers `end`).
      if (lastUtterance && onAllDone) {
        lastUtterance.addEventListener('end', onAllDone, { once: true });
        lastUtterance.addEventListener('error', onAllDone, { once: true });
      } else if (onAllDone) {
        // Fallback if for some reason speak() never queued anything
        setTimeout(onAllDone, 0);
      }
    }

    /** Stop any in-progress narration and revert the active button. */
    function stopNarration() {
      try { window.speechSynthesis.cancel(); } catch {}
      if (currentlyNarratingBtn) {
        currentlyNarratingBtn.textContent = '🔊';
        currentlyNarratingBtn.title = 'Narrate this message';
        currentlyNarratingBtn = null;
      }
    }

    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Per-message 🔊 button (MutationObserver decoration)
    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    function decorateMessageEl(msgEl) {
      if (!msgEl || msgEl._pfNarrateDone) return;
      msgEl._pfNarrateDone = true;

      const btn = document.createElement('button');
      btn.type = 'button';
      btn.className = 'pf-narrate-btn emojiButton';
      btn.textContent = '🔊';
      btn.title = 'Narrate this message';
      // The .emojiButton class (defined upstream around line 1530) already
      // provides opacity:0.5 + cursor:pointer + :hover{opacity:1}. We only
      // override the default <button> UA chrome and add the inline sizing
      // upstream's existing icons use. NO opacity / cursor here — those
      // would just fight the .emojiButton rule.
      btn.style.cssText = 'background:none;border:none;'
                       + 'padding:0;line-height:1;color:inherit;'
                       + 'font-size:0.8rem;margin-left:1rem;'
                       + 'display:flex;align-items:center;';
      btn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
        e.stopPropagation();
        handleNarrateClick(msgEl, btn);
      });

      // Position: immediately after .recomputeButton (the 🔁 regenerate
      // icon). That places the button inside the same unnamed flex
      // wrapper that already holds .recomputeButton + .recomputeWith-
      // AltModelButton + .messageVariantsCtn — visually:
      //
      //   ✏️ 🗑️ 🔁 🔊 🙄 [variants]
      //                 ↑ narrate button lives here
      //
      // Earlier rounds appended to the end of .info, which was AFTER the
      // statusMessage / hiddenFromAi icons — out of the action row.
      // insertBefore(node, null) === appendChild, so the recomputeBtn-is-
      // last-child case is also handled cleanly.
      const recomputeBtn = msgEl.querySelector('.recomputeButton');
      if (recomputeBtn && recomputeBtn.parentNode) {
        recomputeBtn.parentNode.insertBefore(btn, recomputeBtn.nextSibling);
      } else {
        // Defensive: if upstream ever removes .recomputeButton (rare)
        // we still want the button visible somewhere. End-of-.info is
        // not as nicely positioned but is at least in the action row.
        const fallback = msgEl.querySelector('.info') || msgEl;
        fallback.appendChild(btn);
      }
    }

    function findMessageBody(msgEl) {
      return msgEl.querySelector('.messageText, .message-text, .msg-body, .msg-content, .content')
          || msgEl;
    }

    function handleNarrateClick(msgEl, btn) {
      // Toggle: if THIS button is active, stop. If a DIFFERENT button is
      // active, stop it and start this one (most-recent click wins).
      if (currentlyNarratingBtn === btn) {
        stopNarration();
        return;
      }
      stopNarration(); // clears any other narration in flight

      const body = findMessageBody(msgEl);
      const raw = (body.textContent || '').trim();
      if (!raw) return;
      const text = cleanForSpeech(raw);
      if (!text) return;
      const segments = splitForNarration(text, cfg.dualMode);

      currentlyNarratingBtn = btn;
      btn.textContent = '⏹';
      btn.title = 'Stop narration';

      speakSegments(segments, () => {
        // onAllDone — only revert if THIS button is still the active one
        // (a subsequent click on another button might have moved the
        // active narration elsewhere; don't stomp).
        if (currentlyNarratingBtn === btn) {
          btn.textContent = '🔊';
          btn.title = 'Narrate this message';
          currentlyNarratingBtn = null;
        }
      });
    }

    function scanAndDecorate(root) {
      const msgs = root.querySelectorAll('.message, .chat-message, [data-message-id]');
      msgs.forEach(decorateMessageEl);
    }

    function watchMessages() {
      const chatEl = document.getElementById('chatMessagesEl') ||
                     document.querySelector('.chat-messages, .chat-area');
      if (!chatEl) {
        setTimeout(watchMessages, 800);
        return;
      }
      scanAndDecorate(chatEl);
      const obs = new MutationObserver((muts) => {
        if (!cfg.enabled) return;
        for (const m of muts) {
          for (const node of m.addedNodes || []) {
            if (node.nodeType !== 1) continue;
            if (node.matches && node.matches('.message, .chat-message, [data-message-id]')) {
              decorateMessageEl(node);
            } else {
              scanAndDecorate(node);
            }
          }
        }
      });
      obs.observe(chatEl, { childList: true, subtree: true });
    }

    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Settings modal
    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    function openSettingsModal() {
      // Always re-fetch voices when opening — Chrome populates the list
      // asynchronously after first call, and the user might have just
      // installed a new system voice.
      refreshVoices();

      const overlay = document.createElement('div');
      overlay.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;inset:0;z-index:99999;'
                            + 'background:rgba(0,0,0,0.6);display:flex;'
                            + 'align-items:center;justify-content:center;padding:20px;';
      const card = document.createElement('div');
      card.style.cssText = 'background:var(--pf-theme-secondary, #142847);'
                         + 'color:var(--pf-text-color, #e8dcc4);'
                         + 'border:1px solid var(--pf-accent, #d8b36a);'
                         + 'border-radius:8px;padding:18px;max-width:440px;width:100%;'
                         + 'box-shadow:0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.6);'
                         + 'max-height:88vh;overflow-y:auto;';

      const title = document.createElement('div');
      title.textContent = '🔊 Narrator settings';
      title.style.cssText = 'font-weight:700;margin-bottom:12px;'
                          + 'color:var(--pf-accent, #d8b36a);font-size:16px;';
      card.appendChild(title);

      // ---- Master enable toggle ----
      const enableRow = mkToggleRow('Show 🔊 button on messages', cfg.enabled, (v) => {
        cfg.enabled = v;
        saveCfg(cfg);
        // Show / hide existing buttons live
        document.querySelectorAll('.pf-narrate-btn').forEach((b) => {
          b.style.display = v ? '' : 'none';
        });
      });
      card.appendChild(enableRow);

      // ---- Dual-mode toggle ----
      const dualRow = mkToggleRow(
        'Use a different voice for "quoted speech"',
        cfg.dualMode,
        (v) => { cfg.dualMode = v; saveCfg(cfg); refreshDualSection(); }
      );
      card.appendChild(dualRow);

      // ---- Narrator section ----
      const narratorHdr = mkSectionHeader('Narrator voice');
      card.appendChild(narratorHdr);

      const narratorVoiceSel = mkVoiceSelect(cfg.narratorVoiceURI, (uri) => {
        cfg.narratorVoiceURI = uri; saveCfg(cfg);
      });
      card.appendChild(narratorVoiceSel.row);

      const narratorRate = mkSliderRow('Rate', cfg.narratorRate, 0.5, 1.5, 0.05, (v) => {
        cfg.narratorRate = v; saveCfg(cfg);
      });
      card.appendChild(narratorRate);

      const narratorPitch = mkSliderRow('Pitch', cfg.narratorPitch, 0.5, 1.5, 0.05, (v) => {
        cfg.narratorPitch = v; saveCfg(cfg);
      });
      card.appendChild(narratorPitch);

      // ---- Character section (only meaningful when dualMode) ----
      const characterSection = document.createElement('div');
      characterSection.style.cssText = 'margin-top:8px;'
                                     + 'border-top:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08);'
                                     + 'padding-top:8px;';

      const characterHdr = mkSectionHeader('Character voice (for quoted speech)');
      characterSection.appendChild(characterHdr);

      const characterVoiceSel = mkVoiceSelect(cfg.characterVoiceURI, (uri) => {
        cfg.characterVoiceURI = uri; saveCfg(cfg);
      });
      characterSection.appendChild(characterVoiceSel.row);

      const characterRate = mkSliderRow('Rate', cfg.characterRate, 0.5, 1.5, 0.05, (v) => {
        cfg.characterRate = v; saveCfg(cfg);
      });
      characterSection.appendChild(characterRate);

      const characterPitch = mkSliderRow('Pitch', cfg.characterPitch, 0.5, 1.5, 0.05, (v) => {
        cfg.characterPitch = v; saveCfg(cfg);
      });
      characterSection.appendChild(characterPitch);

      card.appendChild(characterSection);

      function refreshDualSection() {
        const dim = !cfg.dualMode;
        characterSection.style.opacity = dim ? '0.4' : '1';
        characterSection.style.pointerEvents = dim ? 'none' : '';
      }
      refreshDualSection();

      // ---- Action row (Test, Stop, Close) ----
      const actions = document.createElement('div');
      actions.style.cssText = 'display:flex;gap:8px;margin-top:14px;';

      const testBtn = mkActionBtn('🔊 Test', () => {
        // Test sentence with both narrator prose AND quoted speech so
        // the user hears the dual-narrator effect immediately.
        const sample = 'She paused at the threshold. "Hello, traveler," '
                     + 'she said softly. "I\'ve been waiting for you."';
        const segs = splitForNarration(sample, cfg.dualMode);
        speakSegments(segs);
      });
      actions.appendChild(testBtn);

      const stopBtn = mkActionBtn('⏹ Stop', () => stopNarration(), 0.75);
      actions.appendChild(stopBtn);

      const closeBtn = mkActionBtn('Close', () => {
        stopNarration();
        overlay.remove();
      });
      closeBtn.style.marginLeft = 'auto';
      closeBtn.style.background = 'var(--pf-accent, #d8b36a)';
      closeBtn.style.color = 'var(--pf-theme-primary, #0d1b2e)';
      closeBtn.style.fontWeight = '600';
      actions.appendChild(closeBtn);

      card.appendChild(actions);

      overlay.appendChild(card);
      // Click backdrop = close (matches upstream modal behavior elsewhere)
      overlay.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
        if (e.target === overlay) { stopNarration(); overlay.remove(); }
      });
      document.body.appendChild(overlay);
    }

    // ---- Modal control builders (kept inline — no shared util module) ----

    function mkToggleRow(label, initial, onChange) {
      const row = document.createElement('label');
      row.style.cssText = 'display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;cursor:pointer;'
                        + 'margin-bottom:8px;font-size:13px;';
      const inp = document.createElement('input');
      inp.type = 'checkbox';
      inp.checked = !!initial;
      inp.addEventListener('change', () => onChange(inp.checked));
      const txt = document.createElement('span');
      txt.textContent = ' ' + label;
      row.appendChild(inp);
      row.appendChild(txt);
      return row;
    }

    function mkSectionHeader(text) {
      const h = document.createElement('div');
      h.textContent = text;
      h.style.cssText = 'font-size:12px;font-weight:600;opacity:0.75;'
                      + 'margin:6px 0 4px;letter-spacing:0.02em;';
      return h;
    }

    function mkVoiceSelect(initialUri, onChange) {
      const row = document.createElement('div');
      row.style.cssText = 'margin-bottom:6px;';
      const sel = document.createElement('select');
      sel.style.cssText = 'width:100%;padding:5px 6px;font-size:12px;'
                        + 'background:var(--pf-theme-primary, #0d1b2e);'
                        + 'color:inherit;'
                        + 'border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.14);'
                        + 'border-radius:4px;box-sizing:border-box;';

      function refill() {
        sel.innerHTML = '';
        const optDefault = document.createElement('option');
        optDefault.value = '';
        optDefault.textContent = '(browser default)';
        sel.appendChild(optDefault);
        // Sort: prefer English voices first, then by name
        const sorted = voicesCache.slice().sort((a, b) => {
          const ae = /^en/i.test(a.lang || '') ? 0 : 1;
          const be = /^en/i.test(b.lang || '') ? 0 : 1;
          if (ae !== be) return ae - be;
          return (a.name || '').localeCompare(b.name || '');
        });
        for (const v of sorted) {
          const o = document.createElement('option');
          o.value = v.voiceURI;
          o.textContent = `${v.name} — ${v.lang}${v.localService ? '' : ' (remote)'}`;
          if (v.voiceURI === initialUri) o.selected = true;
          sel.appendChild(o);
        }
      }
      refill();

      // If voices weren't loaded yet, refill when they arrive
      try {
        window.speechSynthesis.addEventListener('voiceschanged', () => {
          refreshVoices();
          refill();
        }, { once: true });
      } catch {}

      sel.addEventListener('change', () => onChange(sel.value));
      row.appendChild(sel);
      return { row, sel };
    }

    function mkSliderRow(label, initial, min, max, step, onChange) {
      const wrap = document.createElement('div');
      wrap.style.cssText = 'margin-bottom:6px;';
      const lblRow = document.createElement('div');
      lblRow.style.cssText = 'display:flex;justify-content:space-between;'
                           + 'font-size:11px;opacity:0.7;margin-bottom:2px;';
      const lblTxt = document.createElement('span');
      lblTxt.textContent = label;
      const lblVal = document.createElement('span');
      lblVal.textContent = Number(initial).toFixed(2);
      lblRow.appendChild(lblTxt);
      lblRow.appendChild(lblVal);
      const inp = document.createElement('input');
      inp.type = 'range';
      inp.min = String(min);
      inp.max = String(max);
      inp.step = String(step);
      inp.value = String(initial);
      inp.style.width = '100%';
      inp.addEventListener('input', () => {
        const v = Number(inp.value);
        lblVal.textContent = v.toFixed(2);
        onChange(v);
      });
      wrap.appendChild(lblRow);
      wrap.appendChild(inp);
      return wrap;
    }

    function mkActionBtn(label, onClick, opacity) {
      const b = document.createElement('button');
      b.type = 'button';
      b.textContent = label;
      b.style.cssText = 'padding:6px 12px;cursor:pointer;font-size:12px;'
                     + 'background:rgba(255,255,255,0.08);'
                     + 'color:inherit;'
                     + 'border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.14);'
                     + 'border-radius:4px;'
                     + (opacity ? `opacity:${opacity};` : '');
      b.addEventListener('click', onClick);
      return b;
    }

    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Inject "🔊 narrator settings…" entry into #threadOptionsPopup
    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    function injectOptionsMenuEntry() {
      if (document.getElementById('pf-narrator-settings-mi')) return; // idempotent
      const popup = document.getElementById('threadOptionsPopup');
      if (!popup) return false;
      const btn = document.createElement('button');
      btn.id = 'pf-narrator-settings-mi';
      btn.type = 'button';
      btn.style.textAlign = 'left';
      btn.textContent = '🔊 narrator settings…';
      btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
        // Close the options popup the same way upstream does (set
        // display:none so it reopens cleanly next time)
        popup.style.display = 'none';
        openSettingsModal();
      });
      popup.appendChild(btn);
      return true;
    }

    function tryInjectMenu(remaining) {
      if (injectOptionsMenuEntry()) return;
      if (remaining > 0) setTimeout(() => tryInjectMenu(remaining - 1), 400);
    }

    // ---- Boot ----
    watchMessages();
    tryInjectMenu(20);

    // Public for programmatic / debug use
    if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
      window.__memHero_narrate = {
        open:  openSettingsModal,
        stop:  stopNarration,
        split: splitForNarration,
      };
    }
  }

  /* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     module: chat/_self_bootstrap.js
     ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  // chat/_self_bootstrap.js
  //
  // Self-init for the eight modules above. Wires them up without needing
  // to touch start() in profile/index.js. When mirroring this work back
  // into the src/ tree, you can either keep this self-bootstrap or move
  // the init calls into start(); both are idempotent.
  //
  // The init calls are deferred until DOMContentLoaded so the DOM is at
  // least parsed. Most modules don't need window.db; the ones that do
  // (character_portraits, reimagine) check for db internally and
  // gracefully retry / no-op until upstream finishes loading.

  (function selfBootstrapHnePorts() {
    function go() {
      try { initCharacterFavicon();   } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
      try { initFreshAnchors();       } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
      try { initCommunityPacks();     } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
      try { initBgSlideshow();        } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
      try { initCharacterPortraits(); } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
      try { initReimagine();          } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
      try { initBugReport();          } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
      try { initNarrate();            } catch { /* non-fatal */ }
    }
    if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
      document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', go, { once: true });
    } else {
      // Defer one tick so we're after any synchronous DOM mutation
      // happening immediately after this script tag.
      setTimeout(go, 0);
    }
  })();

  /* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     module: bootstrap.js
     ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  // bootstrap.js
  //
  // Runs at the end of the bundle. We wait for two things before calling
  // start():
  //
  //   1. The DOM to be at least parsed (readyState !== 'loading').
  //   2. Upstream's Dexie instance (window.db) to be set up AND to have
  //      the schema defined. Touching IndexedDB ourselves before upstream
  //      finishes its version/upgrade dance causes Dexie to error on
  //      subsequent transactions with "storeNames parameter was empty".
  //
  // Not imported by Node tests — only included in the Perchance bundle.

  (function bootstrap() {
    const POLL_MS = 100;
    const TIMEOUT_MS = 30_000;
    const deadline = Date.now() + TIMEOUT_MS;

    function upstreamReady() {
      return typeof window !== 'undefined' &&
             window.db &&
             typeof window.db === 'object' &&
             window.db.characters &&
             typeof window.db.characters.toArray === 'function';
    }

    function tryStart() {
      if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
        document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', tryStart, { once: true });
        return;
      }
      if (!upstreamReady()) {
        if (Date.now() >= deadline) {
          console.warn('[pf] upstream DB never became ready; profile feature inactive');
          return;
        }
        setTimeout(tryStart, POLL_MS);
        return;
      }
      try { start(); }
      catch (e) { console.warn('[pf] start() failed:', e && e.message); }
    }

    tryStart();
  })();

})(window.__perchance_fork__ = window.__perchance_fork__ || {});
</script>
